# C.R.A.F.T. PROMPT — OTOS FRAMEWORK v2.1 UPGRADE  
## Spread-Based Editorial System · Canonical Copy Lock · Evidence Density · No Repetition / No Cramming

## C — CONTEXT

STOP.

The current **OTOS Partner Pack Framework v2 — Empty Styled System** is directionally useful but not yet approved.

It fixed some of the previous chaos by creating page types, fixed slots, variants and word limits. However, it still has structural issues that will cause the same problems again if full copy is inserted too early.

The current framework still risks:

- too many boxes;
- too many repeated caveat areas;
- disclaimers taking premium page space;
- copy cramming;
- weak single-page containment for sections that need spread treatment;
- evidence pages that are too large visually but too light in content;
- extra wording above footers that should not exist;
- headlines that are not big or artistically line-broken enough;
- “does / does not” language that is too blunt and weaker than better wording already developed elsewhere;
- layout decisions being made before the correct canonical copy has been selected.

This task is to upgrade the framework to **v2.1**, not build the final AZ PDF yet.

## Core correction

The framework must become a **premium editorial spread system**, not a box-grid system.

Some sections should be allowed to breathe across **two linked pages**, with one image or motif spreading visually across both pages so the reader feels continuity.

Examples:

- Adult ADHD Wave can become a linked two-page spread.
- Boundary / Governance can become a benefit-led “what OTOS enables” page plus a full boundary reference page.
- Evidence can become a compact source-led evidence spread plus appendix ledger.
- Route Map can become image-led route priority, not eight equal cards unless needed.

## Content authority correction

Do not assume the current AZ copy is final for every page.

The **AZ Master Brief Content Lock v5** remains the AZ source base, but page-level copy must now be checked against the wider OTOS language bank.

The best current wording may come from:

- Dr Gillvray / LAMP brief;
- Angel Brief;
- AZ / NIA brief;
- other current partner PDFs;
- Notion pages;
- GitHub/repo copy;
- previous approved partner-pack wording.

The next final copy must be built from a **Canonical Language Spine**.

No new wording should be invented during layout unless the source documents do not already contain the right locked phrase.

## Important copy principle

Move from feature-only statements to **benefit-led language**.

For example, instead of repeatedly saying:

> OTOS does not diagnose, prescribe, treat or replace services.

Use stronger benefit-led wording such as:

> OTOS gives services a live continuity layer without taking over clinical responsibility.

Then hold the full legal/boundary wording in:

- end boundary page;
- appendix;
- numbered note;
- subtle asterisk/reference marker.

The document should not keep repeating full disclaimers on every page.

## Boundary principle

The boundary must be clear, but it must not dominate premium page real estate.

Use:

- subtle asterisk or reference number on relevant claims;
- one short line where necessary;
- full boundary box/page near the end;
- appendix/legal-style reference for the full version.

The main pages should stay strategic and premium.

## Evidence principle

Evidence pages and appendices should become **denser and more useful**.

The current evidence layout is too visually large for too little evidence. Evidence pages can use smaller but still readable font, tighter table structure, source labels, and more rows.

Add space for more evidence from the wider OTOS source bank.

Evidence pages must support:

- ADHD + addiction comorbidity;
- START study;
- CGL / RCPsych material;
- NDTMS;
- Huntley;
- ADHD Taskforce;
- IAS alcohol harm;
- adult ADHD access restrictions;
- over-25 verified / over-23 forecast distinction;
- medication shortages;
- lisdexamfetamine / Elvanse / Vyvanse context;
- clinical letter as founder anchor;
- off-pathway service proof;
- any better stats already present in Notion / repo / partner packs.

Evidence must be source-led and caveated, but not bloated.

## Design corrections required

The framework v2.1 must:

- remove extra wording above footer unless it is the footer itself;
- lock footer content into a clean footer zone only;
- increase headline size;
- use intentional manual line breaks;
- create strong visual hierarchy between headline, quote, body and evidence;
- remove unnecessary boxes;
- remove repeated boxes that restate the paragraph;
- allow linked two-page spreads;
- allow image backgrounds spanning two pages where appropriate;
- use subtle glass overlays only where they improve readability;
- reduce caveat clutter on main pages;
- move detailed caveats to end boundary / appendix;
- make evidence pages compact and high-value;
- stop using boxes as the default answer.

## R — ROLE

You are an elite editorial design-system architect, Canva template designer, healthcare strategy-pack art director, copy-governance editor and evidence-pack production designer.

You understand:

- premium A4 editorial spread systems;
- linked two-page layouts;
- Canva production constraints;
- design-token discipline;
- benefit-led proposition language;
- copy governance;
- avoiding repetition;
- high-density evidence pages;
- footer discipline;
- endnote/disclaimer systems;
- pharma/NHS/investor-facing document structure.

You are not building the final AZ PDF yet.

You are upgrading the framework and planning the canonical copy merge.

### Assistant output 51 — 2026-06-03T00:26:00.438156+00:00 — gpt-5-4-thinking

Yes — **absolutely**. That is the right instinct.

The framework is getting better, but it still risks feeling too **templated** unless we deliberately control **pacing**.

## I agree with all three points

### 1. Add **one break page**
Yes — one **full-image break page** in the middle is a very good idea.

It gives the reader a pause, resets the rhythm, and makes the document feel more editorial and premium.

### 2. Avoid the same page style back-to-back
Yes — we should **hard rule** this.

If two pages in a row use the same structure, the document starts to feel repetitive and mechanical.

### 3. Images should not always be boxed
Yes — very important.

Some images should be:

- full-page background images
- full-bleed top-half images
- spread-across-two-pages images
- edge-to-edge hero images
- only sometimes contained in rounded image frames

If every image sits in the same rounded box, the whole pack will feel formulaic.

---

# My recommendation

## Best place for the break page

Put it **after the problem section and before the solution section**.

So:

- Founder / Clinical / Off-pathway / Adult ADHD Wave / Cost Chain
- then the **break page**
- then:
  - Three-Pronged Model
  - Medicine-Plus-Continuity
  - Connected Pathway
  - Why AZ
  - Route Map

That means the break page becomes the pivot:

> **From problem → to possibility**

That is the strongest editorial moment.

## What the break page should be

A **single full-image page** with either:

### Option A — no text at all
Pure image.
Very premium.
Very confident.

### Option B — one short line only
For example:

- **The pattern is real.**
- **The pathway is missing.**
- **Now build the layer that holds it.**
- **Out The Other Side.**
- **The break in the pathway is where OTOS begins.**

I would lean toward **one short line only**, not a paragraph.

---

# New design rules to add

## Page rhythm rule
No two consecutive pages should use the same page structure unless there is a deliberate spread pair.

Example of good rhythm:

1. Full hero cover  
2. Clean contents / navigation  
3. Image-led declaration  
4. Founder proof hero  
5. Clinical anchor split layout  
6. Off-pathway text/image balance  
7. Adult ADHD wave spread  
8. Dark cost-chain page  
9. **Break page — full image**
10. Three-pronged model  
11. Full-background image + overlay text  
12. Connected Pathway  
13. Route map  
14. Demonstrator  
15. Evidence spread  
16. Commercial  
17. Boundary benefit  
18. Boundary reference  
19. Closing ask  
20–21. Appendices

## Image treatment rule
Use **four image modes** only, and alternate them intentionally:

1. **Full-bleed background image**
2. **Top-band / top-half hero image**
3. **Contained editorial image frame**
4. **Two-page linked spread image**

That stops every page looking the same.

## Full-background rule
At least **3–4 pages** in the pack should use the image as the actual page background, with text overlaid using subtle glass panels only where needed.

Best candidates:

- Cover
- Founder Proof
- Break Page
- Closing Ask
- possibly Why AZ or Connected Pathway

---

# What to tell Claude now

Use this add-on instruction:

---

## ADDITION TO FRAMEWORK v2.1

Add the following rules to the OTOS Partner Pack Framework v2.1:

### 1. Break page
Include **one full-image break page** positioned after the problem sequence and before the solution sequence.

Recommended location:
- after Hidden Cost Chain
- before Three-Pronged Model

Purpose:
- reset pacing
- create emotional/editorial pause
- transition from problem to solution

Break page rules:
- full-page image
- no box grid
- no body copy
- optional one-line statement only
- no footer clutter
- premium, calm, cinematic feel

### 2. Anti-repetition page rhythm rule
No two consecutive pages may use the same structural layout unless they are intentionally designed as a linked spread pair.

Vary:
- full-bleed hero
- split layout
- dark feature page
- contained image page
- two-page spread
- evidence/table layout
- text-led premium page

The deck must feel visually varied all the way through.

### 3. Image treatment variety rule
Images must not always be boxed.

Use a controlled mix of:
- full-page background images
- top-half / top-band hero images
- contained editorial image frames
- two-page spread images

At least 3 pages must use the image as the background.

### 4. Full-background page candidates
Recommended pages for full-background or near-full-background treatment:
- Cover
- Founder Proof
- Break Page
- Closing Ask
- optionally Why AstraZeneca or Connected Pathway

### 5. Box restraint rule
Do not default to boxes.

Only use a box when it adds a distinct function:
- caveat
- stat
- evidence note
- pull quote
- action prompt

Never repeat paragraph meaning in a box.

### 6. Output requirement
Update the framework so the page/spread library clearly marks:
- which pages are full-background
- which are contained-image
- which are spread pages
- where the break page sits

Do not insert full copy yet.

### Assistant output 67 — 2026-06-03T12:11:00.308180+00:00 — gpt-5-5-thinking

Yes — Claude **can** help, but only if the job is framed as **controlled population and enhancement**, not “rewrite the PDFs”.

You have already done the hard work. The problem is not lack of copy. It is that the best lines are scattered across the PDFs, Notion, repo/site versions, and the newer NIA/AZ work. So the safest method is:

**One route at a time. Existing PDF as the base. Enhance only from named sources. No fresh rewriting unless a slot is empty or the existing wording is clearly weak.**

The empty master template is now the shell: 25 pages, route-agnostic, placeholder-only, clean numbering, no AZ/NIA copy baked in. That means it is safe to populate later without fighting the layout again. fileciteturn38file0L1-L1

## Best approach

### Phase 1 — Build one “route copy pack” at a time

For each PDF, give Claude only:

1. the existing PDF / current copy for that route;
2. the strongest related Notion/page/repo source;
3. the shared “best lines” source, such as BEST COPY ANGELS;
4. the NIA paragraph / NIA insertion rules;
5. the empty master template.

Do **not** give it the whole universe every time.

### Phase 2 — Claude does a placement plan, not a rewrite

For each route, Claude must produce:

| Template page | Existing copy to use | Better line to add | Source | Delete repetition? | Dean decision needed? |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|

This stops it from wandering.

### Phase 3 — Only then it creates the populated copy

Once the placement plan looks right, Claude creates **page-by-page copy blocks** for the template.

Not final PDF yet. Just copy blocks.

### Phase 4 — Populate the template

Only after the copy blocks are right, it drops them into the v2.3 template.

### Phase 5 — QA

Each PDF is checked for:

- same shared OTOS language;
- route-specific opening clarity;
- NIA included correctly;
- no repeated caveats;
- no repeated paragraph/box meaning;
- clear ask;
- no overclaiming;
- no route confusion;
- no content invented.

## Suggested order

Start where the system has most maturity:

1. **Angel Brief** — create the first gold-standard outreach PDF.
2. **Champion Brief** — improve the “responding to your interest” opening.
3. **Dr Claire / LAMP** — likely strongest mature partner route.
4. **Alcohol Liaison** — use the bedside story and “Next time we meet…” line.
5. **CGL / CRS**
6. **Public Health / Local Authority**
7. **AZ / NIA** — last or separate, because it has the new category/ownership angle.

## How NIA should enter each PDF

NIA should be present across the pack, but not the same weight everywhere.

| Route | NIA weight | How to use it |
|---|---:|---|
| AZ | Heavy | Category ownership / first mover / medicine-plus-continuity |
| Angel | Medium-heavy | Strategic category and investment thesis |
| Champion | Medium | Simple, memorable “this is the hidden pattern” |
| Clinical partner | Medium | Proposed terminology, clinically careful |
| Alcohol Liaison | Light-medium | “The pattern at the bedside” |
| CGL / CRS | Medium | Addiction services already see the pattern |
| Public Health | Medium | Hidden cost chain / population risk |

Standard rule:

> **NIA is proposed terminology, not an official diagnosis. It names the pattern OTOS is built to test: addiction in the foreground, unresolved ADHD underneath, and a missing continuity layer between services.**

## The master prompt for Claude

Use this in the new window when you’re ready.

```text
C.R.A.F.T. PROMPT — OTOS OUTREACH PDF POPULATION SYSTEM
One route at a time · enhance existing copy · do not rewrite · use only named sources

C — CONTEXT

We have finished the empty OTOS Outreach PDF Template v2.3.

This template is the layout shell only.

Do not change the template structure.

Do not redesign the PDF.

Do not create a new content spine.

Do not create a new master copy page.

Do not rewrite OTOS from scratch.

We are now populating one outreach PDF at a time.

These PDFs are not website pages. They are outreach PDFs sent after:
- a phone call;
- an email bite;
- a warm introduction;
- a direct partner approach.

The purpose of each PDF is to make the recipient understand the route quickly, feel the relevance, and agree to a strategic conversation.

Each route is different, but they must all sound like the same OTOS organism.

The PDFs must share consistent language for:
- what OTOS is;
- what OTOS is not;
- NIA;
- the missing continuity layer;
- founder proof;
- evidence discipline;
- pre-pilot / demonstrator status;
- conversation-first ask.

But each PDF must have its own route-specific opening, emotional hook and ask.

The key rule:

USE EXISTING COPY FIRST.

This is not a rewrite job.

This is a controlled enhancement and placement job.

We are protecting the best writing already produced and adding only the best missing material from named sources.

R — ROLE

You are an elite healthcare outreach PDF editor, copy-governance operator, strategic communications editor, pharma/NHS/VCSE-facing positioning specialist and Canva/PDF population planner.

You are not a freeform copywriter.

You are not inventing a new strategy.

You are not creating another master document.

You are preserving and improving existing OTOS copy.

A — ACTION

Step 1 — Confirm working rules

Confirm:

- the empty template is the layout shell;
- the current route PDF/copy is the base;
- source documents are supporting material only;
- no final wording is approved unless Dean approves it;
- do not call anything “locked” unless Dean says lock it;
- do not rewrite from scratch;
- do not insert content into Canva yet;
- first output is a placement plan only.

Step 2 — Source inventory

Create a short source inventory.

Use only the sources provided in this window.

Classify each source as:

- route base;
- shared language source;
- evidence source;
- NIA source;
- visual/template source;
- do-not-use / negative reference.

Do not search beyond the provided sources unless explicitly told.

Step 3 — Route diagnosis

For the selected PDF route, diagnose:

1. What is already strong.
2. What is unclear at the beginning.
3. Where it gets woolly.
4. Where it repeats itself.
5. Where it needs stronger route-specific relevance.
6. Where NIA should be added.
7. Where evidence should be referenced.
8. What should not be touched.

Keep this short.

Step 4 — Create a page-by-page placement plan

Do not write the final copy yet.

Map the selected route into the OTOS Outreach PDF Template v2.3.

Use this table:

| Template page | Page purpose | Existing route copy to keep | Better source line to add | NIA insertion? | Evidence insertion? | Delete/reduce repetition? | Dean decision needed? |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

Rules:

- Use existing route copy as the base.
- Add better lines only from named sources.
- If a phrase from another source is stronger, suggest it.
- Do not paraphrase unless needed for fit.
- Do not invent new claims.
- If unsure, mark “Dean decision needed.”
- Do not populate Canva yet.

Step 5 — Shared language consistency check

Check that the route uses consistent OTOS language for:

- OTOS as a non-clinical continuity layer;
- wraps / supports / never replaces services;
- surfaces signals, partner teams decide;
- known to services is not the same as held;
- continuity thread;
- pre-pilot / demonstrator / conversation-first ask;
- evidence candidates, not guaranteed outcomes;
- NIA as proposed terminology.

Return:

| Language area | Use current wording? | Better source wording? | Recommendation |
|---|---|---|---|

Step 6 — NIA insertion rule

Do not force NIA everywhere.

For the selected route, decide the correct NIA weight:

- light;
- medium;
- heavy.

Then place NIA in one of these ways:

Light:
One short paragraph or footnote.

Medium:
One short paragraph plus one route-specific line.

Heavy:
Dedicated NIA page or major section.

Default NIA wording to adapt only if appropriate:

“NIA — Neurologically Informed Addiction — is proposed terminology, not an official diagnosis. It names the pattern OTOS is built to test: addiction in the foreground, unresolved or undertreated ADHD underneath, and a missing continuity layer between services.”

Step 7 — Repetition removal

Identify repeated meaning.

Return:

| Repeated point | Appears where | Keep | Remove / merge | Reason |
|---|---|---|---|---|

Hard rules:

- Do not say the same point in a paragraph and a box.
- Do not repeat full disclaimers on every page.
- Use subtle reference markers where needed.
- Full boundary belongs near the end or appendix.
- Boxes are for bullets, stats, quotes, evidence labels, asks or boundary references only.

Step 8 — Route-specific ask

Create the strongest ask for this route.

The ask must be:

- specific;
- low-burden;
- conversation-led;
- not procurement unless the route requires it;
- not vague “please advise”;
- not overclaiming.

Return 3 options:

1. Soft ask.
2. Direct ask.
3. Strong strategic ask.

Step 9 — Produce copy blocks only after placement plan approval

Do not produce final copy blocks unless Dean says:

APPROVE PLACEMENT PLAN — PRODUCE COPY BLOCKS

When approved, produce:

| Template page | Headline | Subhead/body | Quote/stat/card copy | Evidence/source note | Footer/status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|

Step 10 — Stop

Do not populate Canva.

Do not export PDF.

Do not create final design.

Stop after the placement plan unless Dean explicitly approves the next step.

F — FORMAT

Output exactly:

# OTOS Outreach PDF Population Plan — [ROUTE NAME]

## 1. Working rule confirmation

## 2. Source inventory

| Source | Role | Use / do not use |
|---|---|---|

## 3. Route diagnosis

## 4. Page-by-page placement plan

| Template page | Page purpose | Existing route copy to keep | Better source line to add | NIA insertion? | Evidence insertion? | Delete/reduce repetition? | Dean decision needed? |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

## 5. Shared language consistency check

| Language area | Use current wording? | Better source wording? | Recommendation |
|---|---|---|---|

## 6. NIA insertion recommendation

## 7. Repetition removal list

| Repeated point | Appears where | Keep | Remove / merge | Reason |
|---|---|---|---|---|

## 8. Route-specific ask options

## 9. What not to touch

## 10. Next instruction needed from Dean

T — TARGET AUDIENCE

Immediate reader: Dean Butler.

Later users:
- Canva/PDF population window;
- Claude copy support;
- Gareth or staff sending PDFs;
- partner-specific recipients.

Tone:
- strict;
- controlled;
- no broad invention;
- no rewriting from scratch;
- protective of Dean’s best existing work;
- practical;
- direct;
- consistency-first.
```

The **Public Health Cost Case** is probably the strongest cost/commissioning version. That document is better at money logic than the Angel copy. fileciteturn44file0

The **Clinical Letter Context** is the safest, cleanest clinical-boundary version. It correctly puts care first, says OTOS is separate, then makes the clinical-letter point without making it feel like a pitch. That is the model for any wording around the psychiatrist’s letter. fileciteturn44file3

The **CGL/CRS Brief** is one of the best emotionally. “You held the thread for me once. Let’s do it on purpose this time,” “the relapse the treatment system created itself,” and “the programme called it a character defect. I knew it wasn’t” are exactly the kind of truth the NIA version must not flatten into corporate language. fileciteturn44file4

The **Alcohol Liaison Brief** is the best bedside-pathway version. “The bedside sees the pattern before the pathway does,” “You did not fail me. You showed me where the route was missing,” and “first, make the moment visible; later, route it faster” are excellent. This is the proof that route-specific wording matters. fileciteturn44file5

The **RCE Brief** and **Mind/WorkWell/Therapy Brief** are where the “medication made the work land” truth is strongest. RCE has the crucial line: “Medication does not do the recovery work. But it creates the conditions where the work can finally land.” Mind/WorkWell has the same sequence in therapy form: “Therapy worked — once the brain had what it needed.” These are essential to the NIA retrofit. fileciteturn44file6 fileciteturn44file7

The **Dr Claire / LAMP Brief** is probably the best “strategic pressure-test” version. “Critique the seam, not the company” is excellent. It also explains complementary infrastructure properly: OTOS is not another content product; it wraps around LAMP/RCE/waiting-well support. This is the language to stop the NIA version sounding like it’s trying to own everything. fileciteturn44file8

The **Champion Brief** has the best low-pressure invitation tone. It is not the strongest strategic explanation, but it is excellent on consent, privacy, “not endorsement,” and “a one-line reply is the whole ask.” That tone matters for any high-sensitivity recipient. fileciteturn44file1

## Where the NIA/AZ work has gone slightly off

The NIA work is strategically powerful, but it risks becoming **too self-writing** and **too category-first**. The existing PDFs prove the better hierarchy:

1. **Dean’s lived route is the truth.**

3. **Medication stability changed what could finally land.**
4. **ADHD-driven addiction is the pattern.**
5. **NIA is the name Dean is now giving that pattern.**
6. **OTOS Continuity is the infrastructure that holds it.**

So NIA should not replace the existing language everywhere. It should sit as the **named category layer** above “ADHD-driven addiction,” not erase the better human phrasing already in the PDFs.

```text
Key logic:

- This is not a new cost.
- The system is already spending reactively.
- The cost moves across budget lines.
- The Angel opportunity is to fund the proof-of-question.
- A small cheque buys speed, evidence and founder time.
- The demonstrator exists to replace modelling with local evidence.
- Macro cost + founder proof + bounded local demonstrator = investable early opportunity.

STEP 11 — POLICY ALIGNMENT

Mention these once in the main body or evidence/policy page.

Do not overstate them.

Do not imply endorsement, commissioning or approval.

Required mentions:

1. NHS 10 Year Health Plan:
- hospital to community;
- analogue to digital;
- sickness to prevention.

2. 250 NHS Community Hubs:
Mention that OTOS is designed to operate naturally with current and emerging community health infrastructure.

3. DHSC addiction healthcare / addiction technology / structural fragmentation:
Mention that addiction, ADHD, hospital, community and recovery services each see part of the person, and OTOS tests whether a light continuity layer can make the missing thread visible.

STEP 12 — ASK / INVESTABILITY

The ask must be funder-facing but not desperate.

Include:
- small first cheque;
- 9–12 months founder protection if appropriate;
- bounded 12-week demonstrator;
- structure open: SAFE, ASA, structured grant-with-rights, angel syndicate, or other route shaped by the conversation;
- no blanket fundraising;
- next step: 30-minute strategic conversation with Dean and Gareth.

Use the existing Angel investability summary if still strong:

Category.
Founder.
Region.
Optionality.
Risk posture.
Proof path.

Update “Category” to include NIA / ADHD-driven addiction, not generic continuity.

STEP 13 — BOUNDARY LANGUAGE

Use one clean boundary section near the end.

Do not repeat full disclaimers throughout.

Boundary must say:

- OTOS is early-stage and pre-pilot.
- NIA is proposed terminology, not an official diagnosis.
- OTOS does not diagnose, prescribe, treat addiction, deliver therapy, replace services, make clinical decisions or act as an EPR.
- OTOS surfaces signals; partner teams make decisions.
- Clinical letter is medical correspondence, not endorsement.
- Published figures are evidence candidates, not OTOS outcomes.
- Modelling is potential, not guaranteed savings.
- Any future work is subject to governance, consent, data protection and qualified clinical leadership.

STEP 14 — STYLE REQUIREMENTS

Use the new template’s stronger editorial style.

Main-page copy must not feel crammed.

No repeated boxes.

No paragraph repeated as a box.

No tiny text.

Use:
- strong headlines;
- short subheads;
- few powerful evidence cards;
- one strong quote/card per page where needed;
- appendix for dense material.

Protect the best voice from the current PDFs:
- direct;
- human;
- serious;
- founder-led;
- not corporate mush;
- not vague “innovation” language.

STEP 15 — PRODUCE THE ANGEL BRIEF vNEXT

1. A short change summary.
2. A page-by-page Angel Brief vNext copy table.
3. A design/population note for each page.
4. A source/evidence note for each page.
5. A “what was preserved from original Angel” list.
6. A “what was borrowed from other PDFs/founder story” list.
7. A “what was removed or softened” list.
```

```text
Page label:
THE OPPORTUNITY

Headline:
A founder-built intervention for ADHD-driven addiction — and a small test of a much larger category.

Intervention box:
Find ADHD-driven addiction upstream → move the person toward the right ADHD clinical route → hold them in OTOS Continuity → help them get out the other side.

Opening body:
Thank you for asking for more. This is a private strategic briefing about a hidden, high-cost cohort the system already sees and already pays for: adults whose addiction, relapse, repeat A&E contact, family breakdown or service drift may be driven by unresolved or undertreated ADHD underneath.

I am not asking you to look at a vague app or a generic “continuity” idea. I am asking you to look at a defined intervention built from lived proof: find ADHD-driven addiction earlier, move the person toward the right ADHD clinical route, wrap them in OTOS Continuity, and test whether engagement, relapse risk, service follow-through and avoidable cost begin to move.

Dean built this because he survived the exact failure it is designed to solve. His clinical letter names the mechanism in writing: adult ADHD complicated by excess alcohol use to self-medicate inadequately managed ADHD symptoms. That is the proof anchor. NIA — Neurologically Informed Addiction — is Dean’s proposed founder-originated name for the pattern. OTOS Continuity is the infrastructure that tests and holds the route.

Benefit grid:
SOLVES:
Clinically known, operationally lost — people seen by multiple services but held by none across the ADHD/addiction gap.

SAVES:
Reactive spend landing in A&E, detox, crisis, rehab, policing, courts, family breakdown and lost productivity.

BENEFITS:
ADHD teams, addiction services, alcohol liaison, VCSE partners, families, funders and the person who is currently falling between them.

CREATES:
A bounded evidence route for NIA / ADHD-driven addiction, with clear learning, consent, audit trail and outcome signals.

SIMPLIFIES:
The handoff between services. Not a new clinical service — a visible thread around services already funded.

INCREASES:
Engagement, follow-through, appointment visibility, support uptake and the chance that recovery work lands at the right time.

REDUCES:
Silent drift, missed handoffs, repeat presentations, wasted support, unsupported waiting and crisis-led re-entry.

HELPS:
Frontline teams see whether the person is still connected without asking them to become ADHD clinicians or carry the whole solution.

CONNECTS:
CPFT ADHD, CGL/CRS, Alcohol Liaison, RCE, Mind/WorkWell, clinical partners and public-health routes around one visible continuity pathway.

Closing line:
The ask is not blanket fundraising. It is a serious strategic conversation about whether a small first cheque, advisory support or route-pressure-test could help prove or stop this properly.

MECHANISM PAGE FIX

Replace the weak “Surface / Hold / De-risked” Page 7 with a full benefit-led mechanism table.

Use this structure:

Headline:
The intervention is simple: make the missing thread visible.

Subhead:
OTOS does not replace services. It makes the handoff, waiting period and drift between them visible enough for humans to act.

Table columns:
OTOS element | What it does | Why it matters / benefit

Rows:
1. Micro check-ins
Simple signals between appointments.
Shows whether someone is still connected or starting to drift.

2. Transition receipts
Records whether a handoff, signpost or referral actually happened.
Stops next steps being assumed when they disappeared into silence.

3. Appointment and pathway reminders
Prompts before and after the moments that usually slip.
Improves follow-through without adding keyworker workload.

4. Partner-shaped support messages
Allows agreed messages in a partner’s own voice where lawful and consented.
Keeps support familiar, human and connected.

5. Drift and silence visibility
Flags no response, missed next step or loss of contact.
Makes relapse risk visible earlier, before the next crisis.

6. Human review queue
Keeps decisions with people, not automation.
Protects clinical boundaries and avoids automated clinical decision-making.

7. Tokenised continuity trail
Holds minimal continuity signals without becoming a new clinical record.
Gives pathway visibility without EPR write-back or NHS record access.

8. Engagement tracking
Shows what was used, ignored, completed or missed.
Turns “we referred them” into “we know whether it held.”

9. Learning report
Summarises what happened across the bounded cohort.
Creates evidence for the next decision: continue, pivot or stop.

Benefit line below table:
The first product is not a cure. It is visibility: the ability to see whether the person is still held between services, before crisis becomes the only signal.

Boundary line:
OTOS surfaces signals. Partner teams make the decisions.

DO NOT DO THIS

Do not replace the benefits table with three abstract bullets.
Do not make “Continuity” the main headline.
Do not bury the intervention under advisory language.
Do not make the opening page about “why now” before explaining what the opportunity is.
Do not remove the NIA definition, but do not let NIA become abstract.
Do not remove the clinical-letter proof anchor.
Do not overclaim outcomes.

OUTPUT ONLY

Return:

1. Replacement Page 2 copy.
2. Replacement mechanism page copy.
3. A short note confirming which original source content was restored.
4. No PDF export.
5. No design work.
6. Stop.
```

```text
CRITICAL RULES

1. Do not change the copy except for tiny line-fit fixes if absolutely necessary.
2. Do not shrink body text to solve layout.
3. If something feels cramped, recompose the page — do not compress the text.
4. Images can be:

   - half-page or top-half hero images;
   - wide cinematic strips;
   - contained framed images;
   - background images with glass-panel / overlay text;
   - side-panel images with text wrapping in clear space.
5. Use the image composition intelligently:
   - if an image has natural dark space, place light text there;
   - if an image has clean negative space, use that for headline/subhead;
   - if an image has strong focal features, do not cover them badly with text;
   - text can sit over the image as long as contrast and readability are strong.
6. Not every page should use the same image mode.
7. Avoid back-to-back pages with the exact same composition.
8. Avoid boxing every image.
9. Avoid leaving large dead lower-page areas unless deliberately minimal.
10. Logo, footer, page number and document label must remain consistent.

VARIETY OF PAGE MODES REQUIRED

Across Pages 1–10, use a varied mix from these modes:

A. Full-bleed hero background with text overlay
B. Top-half image with lower text block
C. Left image / right text split
D. Right image / left text split
E. Full-width cinematic strip
F. Background image with glass-panel copy box
G. Clean stat/evidence page with only a small image strip
H. Dark proof page with contained image
I. Minimal clean page with image accent only

Do not repeat one mode more than twice unless absolutely necessary.

PAGE-BY-PAGE IMAGE DIRECTION

PAGE 1 — Cover
Use IMAGE 01.

PAGE 2 — Opportunity / Read This First

PAGE 6 — National Stakes / Cost Already Paid

PAGE 10 — What Support Unlocks

PAGES 11–13
No heavy image placement for now.
Keep these clean, minimal, elegant and functional.
Boundary page should be disciplined.
Ask page can be mostly typographic.
Appendix page should remain legible and table-led.

SPECIFIC LAYOUT NOTES

- Headlines should not feel trapped in a tiny top box.
- If needed, enlarge the headline zone so the headline can breathe.
- Some pages can have text running over image background, as long as contrast is excellent.
- Use glass / translucent panels sparingly and only when they improve readability.
- Where the image has natural quiet space, let the text sit directly on the image.
- Respect the image features — do not place text over the strongest focal detail unless intentional and readable.
- The whole document should feel like one family, but not one repeated template.

- no copy rewrite done;
- no overflow;

|---|---|---|---|---|

3. A brief note confirming:

- copy not substantively rewritten;
- image variety achieved;
- Pages 11–13 kept minimal.

Then stop.
```

### Assistant output 95 — 2026-06-03T22:18:03.946997+00:00 — gpt-5-5-thinking

Yes — **“billions” can stay**, but not as **“could realise £billions in savings”** on the cover. That risks sounding like a promised return before they have read the evidence. The safer, stronger version is:

> **The downstream cost chain is already measured in billions. The question is whether this cohort can be found earlier, routed better and held long enough to stop that cost compounding.**

That is defendable because the same draft later carries the macro evidence: **£47bn annual UK alcohol and drug economic cost** and **£17bn avoidable annual ADHD economic cost** on the National Stakes page. fileciteturn53file0

Your cover instinct is right though. The image is now doing the work. The cover should be **shorter, sharper and cleaner**. Current problems are mostly grammar and claim-shape:

- “Target this cohort upstream…” should be **“Targeting this cohort upstream…”**
- “£billions in savings” should become **“multi-billion downstream cost chain”** or **“cost chain measured in billions”**
- “lived expereince ad evidence” needs correcting
- “Neurologically-Informed Addiction” should probably be **Neurologically Informed Addiction** without the hyphen, to match NIA usage elsewhere
- The cover has too many sentences now. It should shock, define and pull them in.

## My preferred cover copy

Use this:

```text
Out The
Other Side.

When ADHD is left waiting, addiction steps in — and it does not arrive quietly.

It brings crisis, chaos and cost with it. The system repeatedly treats the addiction, while the underlying ADHD driver is missed.

There is an unrecognised but identifiable, targetable and treatable cohort: people with ADHD-driven addiction. We call the pattern Neurologically Informed Addiction — NIA.

The downstream cost chain is already measured in billions. This brief shows why finding the cohort earlier, routing it to the right clinical door and holding it there could unlock a new evidence-led intervention pathway.

Founder proof, clinical letter and evidence follow.
```

That keeps your power, keeps “billions”, but avoids sounding like guaranteed savings.

## Slightly punchier version

```text
Out The
Other Side.

When ADHD is left waiting, addiction steps in — and it does not arrive quietly.

It brings crisis, chaos and cost with it. The system repeatedly treats the addiction, while the underlying ADHD driver is missed.

This brief is about an unrecognised but identifiable cohort: ADHD-driven addiction. We call the pattern Neurologically Informed Addiction — NIA.

The downstream cost chain is already measured in billions. The opportunity is to find the cohort earlier, route it to the right clinical door and hold it there long enough for the right support to land.

Founder proof, clinical letter and evidence follow.
```

I prefer this second one for the cover. It is cleaner and more investor-readable.

### Assistant output 99 — 2026-06-03T23:18:34.976434+00:00 — gpt-5-5-thinking

Yes — exactly.

The rule is **not** “story over here, solution over there.”  
The rule is:

> **Dean’s story is the evidence trail that reveals the solution.**

So we must make sure the brief still clearly carries the **strategic spine**, even when it is woven through the story.

The rules we were writing to are:

## Strategic spine rules

**1. Need**  
There is a hidden, high-cost cohort already touching multiple services: adults whose addiction, relapse, crisis or drift may be driven by unresolved or undertreated ADHD.

**2. Opportunity**  
This cohort is identifiable, targetable upstream and expensive downstream. The cost chain is already measured in billions, but OTOS is not claiming guaranteed savings.

**3. Founder proof**  
Dean is not just telling a story. His pathway shows the failure pattern: clinically known, operationally lost, repeatedly seen, never held around the ADHD/addiction loop.

**4. Clinical proof anchor**  
The clinical letter names the mechanism: ADHD complicated by alcohol use to self-medicate inadequately managed ADHD symptoms.

**5. Intervention**  
Find ADHD-driven addiction earlier → route the person to the right ADHD clinical door → wrap them in OTOS Continuity → test whether engagement, relapse risk, service follow-through and avoidable cost begin to move.

**6. NIA category**  
Neurologically Informed Addiction is the proposed name for the pattern, not an official diagnosis.

**7. Benefits**  
It must be clear what OTOS does:
solves operational loss, saves reactive spend, connects services, creates evidence, increases engagement, reduces drift, simplifies handoff, helps frontline teams and benefits the person/family/system.

**8. Strategic crossroads**  
The ask is not “fund everything.” It is: help decide which route to test first — Angel, NHS/public, private clinical, AZ/pharma, VCSE ecosystem or hybrid.

**9. Boundary**  
OTOS does not diagnose, prescribe, treat, replace services or make clinical decisions. It surfaces signals; partner teams decide.

**10. Ask**  
A serious conversation, possible advisory support, and possibly a small first cheque to protect founder time and prove or stop the demonstrator properly.

The current Page 2 does include many of these, but we need to make sure the layout/design pass does **not bury them** inside small cards or let the story become decorative. The opening page currently frames the hidden cohort, the founder’s route, the intervention, NIA and the ask, so the bones are there. fileciteturn55file0

### Assistant output 109 — 2026-06-04T10:01:52.123659+00:00 — gpt-5-5-thinking

Yes. You are right.

At the moment it **describes the cohort**, but it still does not land the **opportunity** hard enough. Page 2 says this is “a private briefing about a hidden, high-cost cohort” and explains the intervention, but it does not say clearly enough: **this is the commercial / strategic opportunity, this is why an angel/advisor should care, and this is the route decision you are being invited into**. fileciteturn63file0

What I would put is this.

```text
THE OPPORTUNITY

A new category, a missing route, and a small test that could show whether reactive addiction spend can be moved upstream.

This is a private strategic advisory briefing, not a formal investment memorandum. It asks one question: can we find adults whose addiction is being driven by unresolved or under-treated ADHD earlier, route them to the right clinical door, and hold them long enough for recovery support to land?

Dean built OTOS because he survived exactly that failure. Services saw the crisis; none held the ADHD/addiction thread. His clinical letter now names the mechanism: adult ADHD complicated by alcohol use to self-medicate inadequately managed ADHD symptoms.

We call the pattern NIA — Neurologically Informed Addiction. It is proposed founder-originated terminology, not an official diagnosis. The opportunity is to test whether this cohort can be identified, routed, held and measured through a bounded demonstrator before the cost lands again in A&E, detox, rehab, policing, courts, family breakdown and lost productivity.

The ask is not “fund everything”. It is a route-pressure-test: which first path is safest, fastest and most valuable to prove or stop?
```

That does the missing job. It says:

- **what this is**: private strategic advisory briefing;
- **what the opportunity is**: new category, missing route, upstream test;
- **why Dean**: he survived the failure;
- **what proof exists**: clinical letter;
- **what NIA is**: proposed category name;
- **what OTOS is testing**: identify, route, hold, measure;
- **what the ask is**: help choose the first route.

I would put that **at the very top of Page 2**, replacing the current “Thank you for asking for more…” block. Keep the benefit grid underneath, but make it shorter if needed.

For the cover, I would keep it minimal but adjust the strapline slightly:

```text
ADHD-driven addiction.
```

## What I would put in before Claude touches it

### Cover strapline

```text
A founder-built intervention for a hidden, high-cost cohort:
ADHD-driven addiction.
```

### Page 2 replacement opening

```text
Thank you for asking for more. This is a private strategic advisory briefing about a hidden, high-cost cohort the system already sees and already pays for: adults whose addiction, relapse, repeat A&E contact, family breakdown or service drift may be driven by unresolved or under-treated ADHD underneath.

The opportunity is not to build another generic recovery app. It is to test a defined upstream intervention: identify ADHD-driven addiction earlier, route the person towards the right clinical door, and hold the thread between services long enough to see whether engagement, relapse risk, follow-through and avoidable cost begin to move.

Dean built OTOS because he lived the failure pattern. A documented ADHD diagnosis sat alongside years of A&E, mental-health, addiction and criminal-justice contact — a missed flag in three different colours, on three different records, none talking to each other. His clinical letter now names the mechanism: adult ADHD, previously complicated with excess alcohol use to self-medicate inadequately managed ADHD symptoms.

We call the pattern NIA — Neurologically Informed Addiction. It is proposed founder-originated terminology, not an official diagnosis. For this brief, NIA is the category signal; OTOS Continuity is the non-clinical infrastructure that tests whether the missing thread can be held.

The ask is not blanket fundraising. It is a route-pressure-test: which first path is safest, fastest and most valuable to prove or stop — Angel/funder, NHS/public, private clinical, AZ/pharma, VCSE ecosystem or a staged hybrid?
```

### Page 2 benefit grid, reduced

```text
SOLVES
Clinically known, operationally lost — seen by many services, held by none.

SAVES
Reactive spend landing in A&E, detox, rehab, policing, courts, family breakdown and lost productivity.

CONNECTS
ADHD, addiction, alcohol liaison, VCSE, clinical partners and public-health routes around one visible thread.

CREATES
A bounded evidence route for NIA: consent, audit trail, learning report and outcome signals.

INCREASES
Engagement, follow-through, appointment visibility and the chance recovery work lands in time.

REDUCES
Silent drift, missed handoffs, repeat presentations and unsupported waiting.
```

That finally says **what the opportunity is**.

```text
Body:

Thank you for asking for more. This is a private strategic advisory brief, not a formal investment memorandum. OTOS Life is being built around a hidden, high-cost cohort the system already sees and already pays for: adults whose addiction, relapse, repeat A&E contact, family breakdown or service drift may be driven by unresolved or under-treated ADHD underneath.

The intervention is deliberately simple: find ADHD-driven addiction earlier, route the person towards the right ADHD clinical door, wrap them in OTOS Continuity, and test whether engagement, relapse risk, service follow-through and avoidable cost begin to move.

We call the pattern NIA — Neurologically Informed Addiction. It is proposed founder-originated terminology, not an official diagnosis. ADHD-driven addiction is the plain-English pattern; NIA is the name; OTOS Continuity is the non-clinical infrastructure underneath.

Dean built this because he lived the pathway failure. A documented ADHD diagnosis sat alongside years of A&E, mental-health, addiction and criminal-justice contact — a missed flag in three different colours, on three different records, none talking to each other. His clinical letter now names the mechanism.

The ask is not blanket fundraising. It is a serious route-pressure-test: which first path is safest, fastest and most valuable to prove or stop — Angel/funder, NHS/public, private clinical, AZ/pharma, VCSE ecosystem or a staged hybrid?

Benefit grid:

SOLVES
Clinically known, operationally lost — seen by many services, held by none.

SAVES
Reactive spend landing in A&E, detox, rehab, policing, courts, family breakdown and lost productivity.

CONNECTS
ADHD, addiction, alcohol liaison, VCSE, clinical partners and public-health routes around one visible thread.

CREATES
A bounded evidence route for NIA: consent, audit trail, learning report and outcome signals.

INCREASES
Engagement, follow-through, appointment visibility and the chance recovery work lands in time.

REDUCES
Silent drift, missed handoffs, repeat presentations and unsupported waiting.

Do not add the old nine-card grid back.

Do not shrink text to make it fit.

If space is tight, adjust layout, not wording.

Keep the founder evidence line:
Dean Butler, Founder — six A&E presentations, two ICU admissions, three private detoxes

Use this final body line:

A large share of adults with untreated or under-treated ADHD self-medicate. The gap between “you’re on the list” and “you’re being held” is where drift, relapse and repeat presentation happen. We are already paying for this failure — just in the worst possible places. Dean did not recover by waiting his turn. He recovered by engineering his own way out, then set out to build the layer the system never had. Founder truth is product truth: lived experience becomes infrastructure when the pathway failure is mapped, bounded and tested.
```

```text
Replace the opening body with:

Thank you for asking for more. This brief is short on purpose. It is a private strategic advisory brief, not a formal investment memorandum. OTOS Life is being built around a hidden, high-cost cohort the system already sees and already pays for: adults falling through the overlap between ADHD, addiction, self-medication, recovery pressure and long clinical waiting lists.

The opportunity is not to build another generic recovery app. It is to test a defined upstream intervention: find ADHD-driven addiction earlier, route the person towards the right clinical door, wrap them in OTOS Continuity, and test whether engagement, relapse risk, service follow-through and avoidable cost begin to move.

NIA — Neurologically Informed Addiction — is the proposed founder-originated name for the pattern. It is not an official diagnosis. ADHD-driven addiction is the plain-English cohort; NIA is the category signal; OTOS Continuity is the non-clinical infrastructure that tests whether the missing thread can be held.

Dean built this because he lived the pathway failure. A documented ADHD diagnosis sat alongside years of A&E, mental-health, addiction and criminal-justice contact — a missed flag in three different colours, on three different records, none talking to each other. His clinical letter now names the mechanism.

The ask is not blanket fundraising. It is a serious route-pressure-test: which first path is safest, fastest and most valuable to prove or stop — Angel/funder, NHS/public, private clinical, AZ/pharma, VCSE ecosystem or staged hybrid?

Use this six-card benefit grid:

SOLVES
Clinically known, operationally lost — seen by many services, held by none.

SAVES
Reactive spend landing in A&E, detox, rehab, policing, courts, family breakdown and lost productivity.

CONNECTS
ADHD, addiction, alcohol liaison, VCSE, clinical partners and public-health routes around one visible thread.

CREATES
A bounded evidence route for NIA: consent, audit trail, learning report and outcome signals.

INCREASES
Engagement, follow-through, appointment visibility and the chance recovery work lands in time.

Silent drift, missed handoffs, repeat presentations and unsupported waiting.

Restore original Angel warmth and clarity.

Headline:

The gap between ADHD and addiction is where people get lost.

Keep quote:

“I am still on the list. I repeatedly touched every service. Not one of them linked the ADHD and held me correctly.”

Keep attribution:

Dean Butler, Founder — six A&E presentations, two ICU admissions, three private detoxes

Use this body:

A large share of adults with untreated or under-treated ADHD self-medicate. Many find their way into addiction services. Few get the ADHD half of the picture identified, assessed or treated in any timely way. The system keeps treating the addiction crisis, but not always the underlying condition driving it.

The gap between “you’re on the list” and “you’re being held” is where drift, relapse, repeat presentation and devastation happen. We are already paying for this failure — just in the worst possible places.

Dean did not recover by waiting his turn. He recovered by engineering his own way out, then set out to build the layer the system never had. Founder truth is product truth: lived experience becomes infrastructure when the pathway failure is mapped, bounded and tested.
```

```text
Replace the opening body with this exact copy:

Thank you for asking for more. This brief is short on purpose. It is a private strategic advisory brief, not a formal investment memorandum. OTOS Life is being built around a hidden, high-cost cohort the system already sees and already pays for: adults falling through the overlap between ADHD, addiction, self-medication, recovery pressure and long clinical waiting lists.

The opportunity is not to build another generic recovery app. It is to test a defined upstream intervention: find ADHD-driven addiction earlier, route the person towards the right clinical door, wrap them in OTOS Continuity, and test whether engagement, relapse risk, service follow-through and avoidable cost begin to move.

NIA — Neurologically Informed Addiction — is the proposed founder-originated name for the pattern. It is not an official diagnosis. ADHD-driven addiction is the plain-English cohort; NIA is the category signal; OTOS Continuity is the non-clinical infrastructure that tests whether the missing thread can be held.

Dean built this because he lived the pathway failure. A documented ADHD diagnosis sat alongside years of A&E, mental-health, addiction and criminal-justice contact — a missed flag in three different colours, on three different records, none talking to each other. His clinical letter now names the mechanism.

The ask is not blanket fundraising. It is a serious route-pressure-test: which first path is safest, fastest and most valuable to prove or stop — Angel/funder, NHS/public, private clinical, AZ/pharma, VCSE ecosystem or staged hybrid?

Use this six-card benefit grid:

SOLVES
Clinically known, operationally lost — seen by many services, held by none.

SAVES
Reactive spend landing in A&E, detox, rehab, policing, courts, family breakdown and lost productivity.

CONNECTS
ADHD, addiction, alcohol liaison, VCSE, clinical partners and public-health routes around one visible thread.

CREATES
A bounded evidence route for NIA: consent, audit trail, learning report and outcome signals.

INCREASES
Engagement, follow-through, appointment visibility and the chance recovery work lands in time.

REDUCES
Silent drift, missed handoffs, repeat presentations and unsupported waiting.

Do not add the old nine-card grid back.

Use attribution:

Dean Butler, Founder — six A&E presentations, two ICU admissions, three private detoxes

Use this body:

A large share of adults with untreated or under-treated ADHD self-medicate. Many find their way into addiction services. Few get the ADHD half of the picture identified, assessed or treated in any timely way. The system keeps treating the addiction crisis, but not always the underlying condition driving it.

The gap between “you’re on the list” and “you’re being held” is where drift, relapse, repeat presentation and devastation happen. We are already paying for this failure — just in the worst possible places.

Dean did not recover by waiting his turn. He recovered by engineering his own way out, then set out to build the layer the system never had. Founder truth is product truth: lived experience becomes infrastructure when the pathway failure is mapped, bounded and tested.

OTOS sits with the direction of travel in national policy: hospital to community, analogue to digital, sickness to prevention. As 250 NHS Community Hubs develop, the missing question is whether the thread between services is held. DHSC addiction-technology direction matters because this is a fragmentation problem as much as a clinical one.

Do not say these are OTOS results.

STEP 13 — PAGE 11 — WHAT SUPPORT UNLOCKS / ADVISORY ASK

Headline:

What a first cheque — or your advice — would unlock.

Use:

A small first cheque or strategic advisory support, sized to reach a credible proof-of-question over the next 9–12 months. Structure is open — SAFE, advance subscription, structured grant-with-rights or angel-syndicate — shaped by the conversation. This is not yet a formal raise, and there is no valuation or fixed terms on the table.

Unlock bullets:
- Protected founder time and a refined demonstrator design.
- Governance and data-protection preparation.
- Qualified clinical-partner scoping.
- Service-specific written support requests.
- Evidence pack and source ledger refinement.
- Route comparison and first-route decision.
- A 12-week demonstrator readiness plan.
- A learning report to continue, pivot or stop.

Add friction / blind spots:

- Dean cannot execute all routes at once — founder time is the bottleneck.
- Clinical governance must be shaped properly before any test.
- The first demonstrator route must be chosen carefully.
- NIA is powerful but must be evidenced safely.
- Partner interest must be converted into written support without overstating endorsement.
- The wrong advisor could pull the company toward their familiar route.
- The right advisor helps choose the framework, not dictate the vision.

Add if it fits:

NIA is not yet a market. That is why the first proof point matters.

STEP 14 — PAGE 12 — BOUNDARY / GOVERNANCE

Use full boundary. Do not shrink into one weak paragraph.

Headline:

The line OTOS holds, stated plainly.

Must include:

STATUS
OTOS is early-stage, pre-pilot and non-clinical. NIA is proposed founder-originated terminology, not an official diagnosis.

WHAT OTOS DOES NOT DO
OTOS does not diagnose ADHD, prescribe, manage medication, treat addiction, deliver therapy, replace NHS, CGL, CPFT, CRS, GP, psychiatry, recovery, VCSE, public-health or crisis services, make clinical decisions, or act as an EPR.

HOW EVIDENCE IS FRAMED
Founder proof is not population proof. Published figures are evidence candidates, not OTOS outcomes. The clinical letter is medical correspondence about Dean, not an endorsement of OTOS. Modelled figures are potential, not guaranteed savings.

PARTNER STATUS
No partner endorsement, approved pilot, procurement, NHS support, CPFT support, CGL support, CRS support, Public Health support, Dr Claire / LAMP support, RCE support, Mind / WorkWell support, AstraZeneca support or clinical endorsement is assumed unless explicitly confirmed in writing.

GOVERNANCE
Any future work would be subject to governance, consent, data protection, safeguarding, qualified clinical leadership and proper commercial/legal review.

Keep:

OTOS surfaces signals; partner teams make the decisions.

STEP 15 — PAGE 13 — NEXT STEP / APPENDIX LEDGER

Headline:

An honest conversation, not pitch theatre.

Use:

If any of this is interesting, the next step is simple: a 30-minute exploratory call with Dean and Gareth. No deck, no pitch theatre — just the founder, the mechanism, the candidate demonstrator, the partner-readiness position, and an honest conversation about which route to test first.

1. Advisory call
Pressure-test the route and the category.

2. Strategic advisor

3. Early backer
Potentially underwrite the bounded proof-of-question if fit is real.

Use:

No onward circulation without permission.

- 23–45% ADHD in addiction-treatment cohorts.
- 61.3% START study.
- £4.07 addiction-continuity comparator.
- £17bn untreated ADHD economic cost.
- £47bn alcohol and drugs economic cost.
- £27.44bn alcohol harm.
```

## Cover support paragraph

```text
Repeated crisis, relapse, despair, family loss and system cost can become locked around an unidentified, unacknowledged driver: untreated or under-treated ADHD.

This brief explains the route Dean had to engineer for himself — and the OTOS intervention designed to find that cohort earlier, route the ADHD clinically, and hold the thread until the right support can finally land.
```

## Page 2 opening replacement

```text
Thank you for asking for more. This brief is short on purpose: a private strategic advisory brief, not a formal investment memorandum.

It is about an unidentified, unacknowledged and high-cost cohort the system already sees and already pays for — adults whose addiction, relapse, repeat A&E contact, family breakdown or service drift may be driven by untreated or under-treated ADHD beneath the visible crisis.

Dean was one of them. His ADHD was already in the story, but it was not held as the driver. After seven years of waiting, he engineered a private route back to proper ADHD treatment. Once medicated correctly, the addiction work, therapy, recovery education and structure finally started to land. That sequence created OTOS.

The opportunity is not another recovery app. It is a bespoke non-clinical infrastructure layer, independent of patient records, that tests whether this cohort can be identified earlier, routed to the right ADHD clinical door, wrapped in continuity, and measured for engagement, relapse risk, follow-through and avoidable cost.

NIA — Neurologically Informed Addiction — is the proposed founder-originated name for the pattern. It is not an official diagnosis. ADHD-driven addiction is the plain-English cohort; NIA is the category signal; OTOS Continuity is the infrastructure that tests whether the missing thread can be held.

The ask is not blanket fundraising. It is a serious route-pressure-test: which first path is safest, fastest and most valuable to prove or stop — Angel/funder, NHS/public, private clinical, AZ/pharma, VCSE ecosystem or staged hybrid?
```

## What Angel v13 already has right

## What is still missing or underpowered

### 1. CGL / Nottingham precedent is not explicit enough

This is the biggest missing Angel point.

The CGL/CRS brief says the cohort is already visible to CGL and CRS, and that CGL has already published practice-development work through Nottingham CVS on ADHD assessment approaches inside substance-use services. It also says “The Team Leaders who held me are one email away.” fileciteturn72file1

The Clinical Letter Context repeats the same point even more cleanly: ADHD is already being recognised inside addiction settings, and OTOS does not duplicate that clinical work — it makes continuity visible between addiction and ADHD pathways so the cohort, once identified, is fast-tracked and held rather than lost. fileciteturn72file2

**Angel v13 only says CGL/CRS recognise the pattern and have been engaged informally. That is not enough.**

Insert this into Page 6 or Page 8:

```text
There is already a live precedent on the addiction side. CGL has published practice-development work through Nottingham CVS’s Practice Development Unit on psychotherapeutic and diagnostic assessment approaches for ADHD within substance-use services — mapping barriers this cohort already presents: symptom overlap, substance-induced symptoms, stigma, missed appointments and chaotic lifestyle.

OTOS does not duplicate that clinical work. It makes continuity visible between the addiction and ADHD pathways, so a cohort already being recognised inside addiction settings can be routed, fast-tracked and held rather than lost.
```

Add this shorter Angel-safe line too:

```text
CGL / CRS is not a cold door: the cohort is already visible there, and the people who held Dean are close enough to help open the conversation — without any endorsement being claimed.
```

### 2. The “not passive waiting / active continuity” idea should be stronger

RCE has one of the clearest system explanations: waiting without structure is where ADHD-driven drift becomes addiction-driven crisis, and RCE turns empty waiting into courses, recovery education, routine, coping tools and something real to do while the clinical pathway catches up. fileciteturn73file0

Angel v13 says waiting and drift, but it does not fully land **active waiting** as a product insight.

Insert into Page 7 Infrastructure:

```text
Waiting is not neutral for this cohort. Waiting without structure is where ADHD-driven drift becomes addiction-driven crisis. OTOS turns waiting from invisible silence into active continuity: a thread of prompts, receipts, human review and partner-shaped support while the clinical pathway catches up.
```

### 3. “The work was not wrong. My brain could not use it yet.”

This is one of the strongest lines from Dr Claire/LAMP and Mind/WorkWell. Mind says therapy worked once the brain had what it needed, and that the sequence mattered: ADHD treated, then the work could finally go in. fileciteturn73file1 Dr Claire’s version is even cleaner: “The work was not wrong. My brain could not use it yet,” followed by the medication-does-not-do-the-recovery-work line. fileciteturn73file2

Angel v13 has some of this, but it is still too thin. It should be a named insight, because it is the bridge between Dean’s life and the intervention.

```text
The work was not wrong. Dean’s brain could not use it yet. Once the ADHD was treated, the same courses, CBT, recovery education and structure became usable. That is the sequence OTOS is trying to protect: identify the driver, hold the person while the clinical route catches up, then make sure the right support is there for the moment it can finally land.
```

### 4. VCSE / therapy / charity “no patient-record access” wedge is missing

Mind/WorkWell contains a very important infrastructure point: charities should not need patient-record access to contribute, and OTOS closes the gap from the other direction with consent-led minimal continuity signals: no therapy content, no EPR write-back, no new clinical record, no NHS record access. fileciteturn73file1

OTOS works from the other direction: consent-led, minimal continuity signals that show whether the support someone was pointed towards actually held — without therapy content, EPR write-back or a new clinical record.

### 5. Dr Claire’s “critique the seam, not the company” should inform the Angel ask

The Dr Claire brief is very strong because it asks for **pressure-testing**, not approval. It says: “I’m not asking you to endorse it. I’m asking you to help me test it properly,” and later, “Critique the seam, not the company.” fileciteturn73file2

### 6. AZ’s “new category / new intervention / new infrastructure” is underused

The AZ master copy is the clearest on the bigger opportunity: **NIA is not yet a market; that is the opportunity**, and the value sits around category, intervention and infrastructure. The life story gives the human proof: Dean had to engineer his own rescue route, fight through A&E/detox access, secure the letter, get reassessed, get Elvanse, and then “within hours” his life made sense. ## My verdict

**Does Angel v13 contain enough of your story?**  
Almost. It contains the spine, the clinical proof, the off-pathway failure and the service team forming. But it still needs the **CGL/Nottingham precedent**, **active waiting**, **VCSE/no-record-access wedge**, **critique-the-seam ask**, and a sharper **“Dean should not have had to engineer his own rescue route”** line.

**Does it contain every major thing an Angel should hear?**  
Not yet. It is 85–90% strategically complete, but these missing pieces are exactly the kind of “this is real, not imaginary” proof an Angel needs.

**Should all missing points be added as new pages?**  
No. Do not blow it up. Add them surgically:

- Page 3: rescue route / founder proof.
- Page 5: work was not wrong / sequence.
- Page 6: CGL Nottingham + Alcohol Liaison + teed-up partner readiness.
- Page 7: active waiting + no patient-record-access wedge.
- Page 11 or 13: critique the seam / advisor role.
- Page 13 or footer note: full evidence ledger held separately.

```text

OTOS does not duplicate that clinical work. It makes continuity visible between the addiction and ADHD pathways, so a cohort already being recognised inside addiction settings can be routed, fast-tracked and held rather than lost.

2. Rescue-route proof:
Dean recovered by engineering a route the system should have had. He had to create the conditions for detox, secure the letter, reach the psychiatrist, restart ADHD treatment and then watch the recovery work finally land. OTOS exists so the next person does not have to design their own rescue route from inside crisis.

3. Work-could-finally-land sequence:

That is the sequence OTOS is trying to protect: identify the driver, hold the person while the clinical route catches up, then make sure the right support is there for the moment it can finally land.

4. Active waiting:
Waiting is not neutral for this cohort. Waiting without structure is where ADHD-driven drift becomes addiction-driven crisis. OTOS turns waiting from invisible silence into active continuity: a thread of prompts, receipts, human review and partner-shaped support while the clinical pathway catches up.

5. VCSE / no-record-access wedge:
VCSE, charity and therapy partners should not need NHS record access to contribute. OTOS works from the other direction: consent-led, minimal continuity signals that show whether the support someone was pointed towards actually held — without therapy content, EPR write-back or a new clinical record.

6. Bedside gap:

7. Angel opportunity:

8. Advisory ask:
```

It is now strategically strong enough, but it still has **visible production defects** that could make an Angel think the work is rushed. The big issue is not the story or strategy now. It is finish.

## Executive verdict

**Content / persuasion:** 8.5–9/10  
**Angel relevance:** 8.5/10  
**Evidence safety:** 9/10  
**Visual polish:** 7/10  
**Sendability today:** **Not quite — one final micro-production pass required.**

It has now done most of what I wanted strategically. It explains the cohort, the founder proof, the clinical letter, the service gap, the team of services forming, the CGL precedent, the route decision, cost, evidence, governance and ask. That is the right Angel landscape briefing. fileciteturn77file0

But I would not let it leave with the current Page 5 and Page 11.

## What is working now

The **cover is finally strong**. It lands the emotional chain: ADHD left waiting → addiction steps in → pain and system cost. The orange “addiction steps in” works. The image is doing its job.

**Page 2 is now much clearer.** It says this is a private strategic advisory brief, explains the unidentified / unacknowledged cohort, names the medication sequence, explains OTOS as non-clinical infrastructure and defines NIA safely.

**Page 4 is one of the strongest pages.** The clinical letter plus RCPsych quote now creates exactly the right anchor: lived proof + external professional logic + demonstrator question.

**Page 6 now does the crucial Angel job.** It says this is not an idea on paper; partner briefs are being prepared; CGL, CRS, Alcohol Liaison, RCE, Mind / WorkWell, Dr Claire / LAMP and Public Health are being approached without claiming endorsement. That is the right “momentum without overclaiming” balance.

**Page 8 is good.** The route table is exactly the kind of thing an Angel/advisor needs: not “give me money blindly,” but “help me choose the safest first route.”

**Page 12 is safe.** The governance / boundary page protects you well.

## What is still not acceptable

### 1. Page 5 still looks unfinished

The two huge bottom boxes with tiny content are the most obvious visual defect in the document. They look like placeholders. This is exactly the kind of thing that makes a polished brief feel unfinished.

Fix: turn those into a compact two-column strip or remove the boxes and make them small inline callouts.

### 2. Page 11 is messy

The bullets on “What Support Unlocks” look broken: arrows, double dots, inconsistent indentation and too much empty space. This page matters because it is effectively the money/advice page. It cannot look weak.

Fix: make it a clean two-column page:
- left: “What support unlocks”
- right: “Friction & blind spots”
No arrows. No double bullets.

### 3. There is no clear funding range

For an Angel/advisory support brief, it is acceptable not to have a valuation, but there should be a **guide funding envelope** if you are asking for help, finance and direction.

Add one safe line on Page 11 or Page 13:

> Working demonstrator budget is expected to sit in the £90k–£150k range, subject to final costing, governance and partner route.

That would make the ask more credible without overcommitting.

### 4. Some text still feels compressed

Pages 6, 7, 11 and 13 are dense. This is not fatal, but the final pass must not shrink text further.

### 5. There are still possible joined-word artefacts

The rendered PDF looks mostly readable, but the parsed text still shows joined artefacts in headings and some body copy. The final export needs a text-extraction check, not just visual check.

## Have we nailed the Angel story?

Mostly, yes.

The Angel now hears:

- Dean lived the pattern.
- ADHD was the unacknowledged driver.
- medication changed what could land.
- the clinical letter names the mechanism.
- the system saw Dean repeatedly but did not hold the thread.
- services are not being blamed.
- partner conversations are being prepared.
- CGL / Nottingham gives a relevant precedent.
- OTOS does not treat, diagnose or prescribe.
- the opportunity is a category / intervention / infrastructure layer.
- the ask is route-pressure-testing, advice and possibly early support.

That is the right story.

The only strategic missing piece is the **funding envelope**. Without even a rough range, an Angel can understand the opportunity but not the practical shape of support.

```text
Use original Angel voice, but state the opportunity cleanly.

Headline:
How to get Out The Other Side.

Opening:

Thank you for asking for more. This brief is short on purpose: a private strategic advisory brief, not a formal investment memorandum.

It is about an unidentified, unacknowledged and high-cost cohort the system already sees and already pays for — adults whose addiction, relapse, repeat A&E contact, family breakdown or service drift may be driven by untreated or under-treated ADHD beneath the visible crisis.

Dean was one of them. His ADHD was already in the story, but it was not held as the driver. After seven years of waiting, he engineered a private route back to proper ADHD treatment. Once medicated correctly, the addiction work, therapy, recovery education and structure finally started to land. That sequence created OTOS.

The opportunity is not another recovery app. It is a bespoke non-clinical infrastructure layer, independent of patient records, that tests whether this cohort can be identified earlier, routed to the right ADHD clinical door, wrapped in continuity, and measured for engagement, relapse risk, follow-through and avoidable cost.

NIA — Neurologically Informed Addiction — is the proposed founder-originated name for the pattern. It is not an official diagnosis. ADHD-driven addiction is the plain-English cohort; NIA is the category signal; OTOS Continuity is the infrastructure that tests whether the missing thread can be held.

The ask is not blanket fundraising. It is a serious route-pressure-test: which first path is safest, fastest and most valuable to prove or stop — Angel/funder, NHS/public, private clinical, AZ/pharma, VCSE ecosystem or staged hybrid?

Benefit grid, six cards only:

SOLVES
Clinically known, operationally lost — seen by many services, held by none.

SAVES
Reactive spend in A&E, detox, rehab, policing, courts, family breakdown and lost productivity.

CONNECTS
ADHD, addiction, alcohol liaison, VCSE, clinical partners and public-health routes around one visible continuity thread.

CREATES
A bounded evidence route for NIA — consent, audit trail, learning report and outcome signals.

INCREASES
Engagement, follow-through, appointment visibility — and the chance recovery work lands in time.

REDUCES
Silent drift, missed handoffs, repeat presentations and unsupported waiting.

- NIA is proposed founder-originated terminology, not an official diagnosis.
- OTOS does not diagnose, prescribe, manage medication, treat addiction, deliver therapy, replace services, make clinical decisions or act as EPR.
- Published figures are evidence candidates, not OTOS outcomes.
- Clinical letter is medical correspondence, not endorsement.
- Modelled figures are potential, not guaranteed savings.
- No partner endorsement is assumed unless explicitly confirmed in writing.
- Any future work is subject to governance, consent, data protection, safeguarding, qualified clinical leadership and proper commercial/legal review.
- OTOS surfaces signals; partner teams make the decisions.
```
