```text
PAGE 02 — THE OPPORTUNITY

Use Image 02 only as subtle atmosphere if it improves the page. Text clarity first.

Headline:
How to get Out The Other Side.

Opening copy:

Thank you for asking for more. This brief is short on purpose: a private strategic advisory brief, not a formal investment memorandum.

It is about an unidentified, unacknowledged and high-cost cohort the system already sees and already pays for — adults whose addiction, relapse, repeat A&E contact, family breakdown or service drift may be driven by untreated or under-treated ADHD beneath the visible crisis.

Dean was one of them. His ADHD was already in the story, but it was not held as the driver. After seven years of waiting, he engineered a private route back to proper ADHD treatment. Once medicated correctly, the addiction work, therapy, recovery education and structure finally started to land. That sequence created OTOS.

The opportunity is not another recovery app. It is a bespoke non-clinical infrastructure layer, independent of patient records, that tests whether this cohort can be identified earlier, routed to the right ADHD clinical door, wrapped in continuity, and measured for engagement, relapse risk, follow-through and avoidable cost.

NIA — Neurologically Informed Addiction — is the proposed founder-originated name for the pattern. It is not an official diagnosis. ADHD-driven addiction is the plain-English cohort; NIA is the category signal; OTOS Continuity is the infrastructure that tests whether the missing thread can be held.

The ask is not blanket fundraising. It is a serious route-pressure-test: which first path is safest, fastest and most valuable to prove or stop — Angel/funder, NHS/public, private clinical, AZ/pharma, VCSE ecosystem or staged hybrid?

Six benefit cards only:

SOLVES
Clinically known, operationally lost — seen by many services, held by none.

SAVES
Reactive spend in A&E, detox, rehab, policing, courts, family breakdown and lost productivity.

CONNECTS
ADHD, addiction, alcohol liaison, VCSE, clinical partners and public-health routes around one visible continuity thread.

CREATES
A bounded evidence route for NIA — consent, audit trail, learning report and outcome signals.

INCREASES
Engagement, follow-through, appointment visibility — and the chance recovery work lands in time.

REDUCES
Silent drift, missed handoffs, repeat presentations and unsupported waiting.

PAGE 03 — FOUNDER PROOF

Use Image 03 as wideEditorialField or halfPageHero.
No boxed image.
No framed photo card.
Image must be architectural.

Headline:
The gap between ADHD and addiction is where people get lost.

Quote:
“I am still on the list. I repeatedly touched every service. Not one of them linked the ADHD and held me correctly.”

Attribution:
Dean Butler, Founder — six A&E presentations, two ICU admissions, three private detoxes.

Copy:
A large share of adults with untreated or under-treated ADHD self-medicate. Many find their way into addiction services; few get the ADHD half identified or treated in time. The gap between “you’re on the list” and “you’re being held” is where drift, relapse and devastation happen. We are already paying for this failure — just in the worst possible places.

Dean recovered by engineering a route the system should have had — creating the conditions for detox, securing the letter, reaching the psychiatrist, restarting ADHD treatment, then watching the recovery work finally land. OTOS exists so the next person does not have to design their own rescue route from inside crisis.

Founder proof is not population proof.

PAGE 04 — CLINICAL ANCHOR / THE COHORT ANCHORED

Use Image 04 as dark proof atmosphere.

Headline:
The thesis, in clinical language.

Quote:
“Adult ADHD, previously complicated with excess alcohol use to self-medicate inadequately managed ADHD symptoms.”

Attribution:
Dr Fagbemi, Consultant Psychiatrist, Psymplicity — diagnostic letter to the CPFT ADHD Team, April 2025.

Copy:
That sentence is the cohort. Not ADHD and alcohol use treated as two separate problems — adult ADHD, complicated by self-medication. Treating the alcohol alone left the driver underneath untouched; recognising and treating the ADHD is what finally let the rest of the work hold.

The letter is medical correspondence about Dean — not an endorsement of OTOS, and not proof that OTOS works — but it documents that the mechanism is real in a lived pathway.

Add standalone dark authority feature:

THE COHORT, ANCHORED

“Significant and comorbid substance misuse should be managed jointly between general adult and addiction services...”

Royal College of Psychiatrists — ADHD in Adults: Good practice guidance (CR235)

So why is this cohort still drifting between services — and why aren’t we actively holding them while they wait? That is what this demonstrator is built to test.

— Dean, founder, OTOS Continuity™

PAGE 05 — PATHWAY FAILURE

Use Image 05 as backgroundAtmosphere or omit if it harms clarity.
No large empty boxes.

Headline:
The system helped me — off-pathway.

Copy:
The services did help — but they had to help him off-pathway, because the named pathway did not exist. Good people improvised a route the system had not built. Help that depends on improvisation cannot be relied on, or scaled.

A person can be clinically known and still operationally lost — known to services is not the same as held between services. The failure was not bad people. It was the absence of a named route.

And one more truth the recovery makes plain: the work was not wrong — Dean’s brain could not use it yet. Once the ADHD was treated, the same courses, CBT, recovery education and structure became usable. That is the sequence OTOS protects: identify the driver, hold the person while the clinical route catches up, then make sure the right support is there for the moment it can finally land.

READY NOW

ADDICTION SIDE
CGL and CRS keyworkers and team leaders already recognise the pattern and have been engaged informally.

CLINICAL SIDE
The diagnostic letter gives the ADHD/self-medication mechanism in clinical language and is being used to progress the NHS pathway.

BEDSIDE SIDE
Alcohol Liaison saw the gap directly: there was nowhere useful to send this pattern once it became visible.

WAITING-WELL SIDE
RCE, Mind / WorkWell, therapy and Dr Claire / LAMP show how active waiting and human connection sit around the clinical pathway.

PUBLIC-HEALTH SIDE
The cost case is framed as discovery, not procurement: can a 50-person, 12-week demonstrator test whether the thread holds?

CGL precedent:
There is already a live precedent on the addiction side: CGL has published practice-development work through Nottingham CVS on ADHD assessment within substance-use services — mapping the very barriers this cohort presents. OTOS does not duplicate that clinical work; it makes continuity visible between the addiction and ADHD pathways, so a cohort already recognised inside addiction settings can be routed and held rather than lost.

Closing line:
No endorsement is claimed, and no partner is being represented as signed up. The point is that the conversation is now teed up — and an advisor or Angel can help shape the safest, fastest first route before the moment is missed.

PAGE 07 — INFRASTRUCTURE

Use Image 07 as subtleTexture only.

Headline:
The intervention is simple:
make the missing thread visible.

Copy:
OTOS does not replace services. It makes the handoff, waiting period and drift between them visible enough for humans to act.

Waiting is not neutral for this cohort: waiting without structure is where ADHD-driven drift becomes addiction-driven crisis — OTOS turns it into active continuity.

Mechanism table:
OTOS ELEMENT | WHAT IT DOES | WHY IT MATTERS

Micro check-ins | Simple signals between appointments | Shows whether someone is still connected or starting to drift
Transition receipts | Records whether a handoff or referral happened | Stops next steps being assumed when they vanished into silence
Pathway reminders | Prompts before and after moments that slip | Improves follow-through without adding keyworker load
Partner-shaped messages | Agreed messages in partner voice, where consented | Keeps support familiar, human and connected
Drift & silence visibility | Flags no response or missed next step | Makes relapse risk visible earlier
Human review queue | Keeps decisions with people, not automation | Protects clinical boundaries
Tokenised continuity trail | Holds minimal continuity signals only | Pathway visibility without EPR write-back
Learning report | Summarises what happened across the cohort | Creates evidence to continue, pivot or stop

Bottom callout:
The first product is not a cure. It is visibility — seeing whether the person is still held between services before crisis becomes the only signal. VCSE, charity and therapy partners need no NHS record access to take part: consent-led, minimal continuity signals only — no therapy content, no EPR write-back, no new clinical record. OTOS surfaces signals. Partner teams make the decisions.

PAGE 08 — STRATEGIC CROSSROADS

Use Image 08 subtly or omit if table needs clarity.

Headline:
Six routes are live.
Which should be tested first?

Copy:
Dean is not pretending the route is already chosen. Six routes are open, each with a different first test — the next real risk is choosing the wrong route first. The Angel is not being asked to choose forever; the Angel is being asked to help pressure-test the first route.

Route table:
A · Angel / funder — First cheque protects founder time + demonstrator — Dilution / wrong early terms — Is this the right first capital, and on what shape?
B · NHS / public — Bounded local demonstrator with governance — Slow procurement; governance load — Is the demonstrator commissionable, and when?
C · Private clinical — ADHD assessment / medication + continuity — Access & equity optics — Does a private partner accelerate or narrow it?
D · AZ / pharma — NIA, medicine-plus-continuity, category — Long cycles; founder distraction — Is the category route worth the time-cost now?
E · VCSE ecosystem — RCE, Mind, WorkWell, Alcohol Liaison, CGL/CRS — Funding fragility — Which partners de-risk the first test?
F · Hybrid — A staged blend of the above — Focus dilution — What is the minimum viable sequence?

Closing:
The near-term test is small either way: a 12-week candidate demonstrator — find the cohort, route to ADHD assessment via qualified channels, wrap in OTOS Continuity, measure candidate change. Small. Surgical. Enough to prove or stop.

PAGE 09 — NATIONAL STAKES / COST CASE

Use Image 09 as wideEditorialField or backgroundAtmosphere.

Headline:
The system is already paying —
in the worst possible places.

Copy:
This is not a new cost. It is a smarter use of money the system is already spending — reactively, repeatedly and across budget lines that rarely speak to each other. The cost does not disappear — it moves to a different budget line. A 50-person, 12-week demonstrator is an evidence budget, not a therapy budget: it exists to replace modelling with locally validated evidence.

Stats:
MACRO BACKDROP
£47bn / year
UK economic cost of alcohol and drugs. HM Government.

THE PRIZE
£17bn
Avoidable annual economic cost of untreated ADHD. NHS ADHD Taskforce, 2025.

LOCAL MODEL
≈£18m / year
Modelled avoidable demand if continuity improves outcomes. OTOS planning estimate — subject to local validation.

PAGE 10 — EVIDENCE / PRIZE / POLICY

Use Image 10 subtly or omit. Evidence clarity first.

Headline:
What getting this right could be worth.

Copy:
The research already says what the founder’s life demonstrated. None of the figures below is an OTOS outcome — they are published evidence candidates and comparators the demonstrator is designed to test locally.

Stats:
23–45%
ADHD prevalence in addiction treatment cohorts
RCPsych

61.3%
Improvement in addictive-disorder outcomes after ADHD diagnosis and treatment
START, 2025

£4.07
Return per £1 in an addiction-continuity comparator
Turning Point / LSE

£17bn
Avoidable annual economic cost of ADHD
ADHD Taskforce 2025

Policy line:
OTOS sits with the direction of travel in national policy — hospital to community, analogue to digital, sickness to prevention. As the planned 250 NHS Community Hubs develop, the missing question is whether the thread between services is held; the DHSC addiction-technology direction matters because this is a fragmentation problem as much as a clinical one.

PAGE 11 — WHAT SUPPORT UNLOCKS

No messy arrows.
No double bullets.
No broken indentation.

Headline:
What a first cheque — or your advice — would unlock.

Opening:
A small first cheque or strategic advisory support, sized to reach a credible proof-of-question over the next 9–12 months. Structure is open — SAFE, advance subscription, structured grant-with-rights or angel-syndicate — shaped by the conversation. This is not yet a formal raise, and there is no valuation or fixed terms on the table.

Angel opportunity line:
This is the Angel opportunity: not just a service idea, but a new category, a new intervention model and a new infrastructure layer. NIA is not yet a market — that is why the first proof point matters.

Funding line:
Working demonstrator budget is expected to sit in the £90k–£150k range, subject to final costing, governance and partner route.

Two-column layout:

LEFT COLUMN — WHAT SUPPORT UNLOCKS
- Protected founder time and a refined demonstrator design.
- Governance and data-protection preparation.
- Qualified clinical-partner scoping.
- Service-specific written-support requests drawn from the partner briefs.
- Evidence pack and route comparison.
- A 12-week readiness plan and learning report to continue, pivot or stop.

RIGHT COLUMN — FRICTION & BLIND SPOTS
- Dean cannot execute all routes at once — founder time is the bottleneck.
- Clinical governance must be shaped properly before any test.
- The first demonstrator route must be chosen carefully.
- Partner interest must become written support without overstating endorsement.
- NIA is powerful but must be evidenced safely.
- The right advisor helps choose the framework, not dictate the vision.

PAGE 12 — BOUNDARY / GOVERNANCE

Headline:
Stated plainly,
held to.

Six balanced cards:
STATUS
OTOS is early-stage, pre-pilot and non-clinical. NIA is proposed founder-originated terminology — not an official diagnosis.

WHAT OTOS DOES NOT DO
Does not diagnose ADHD, prescribe, manage medication, treat addiction, deliver therapy, replace any NHS / CGL / CPFT / CRS / GP / psychiatry / recovery / VCSE / public-health / crisis service, make clinical decisions, or act as an EPR.

HOW EVIDENCE IS FRAMED
Founder proof is not population proof. Published figures are evidence candidates, not OTOS outcomes. The clinical letter is medical correspondence, not an endorsement. Modelled figures are potential, not guaranteed savings.

PARTNER STATUS
No partner endorsement, approved pilot, procurement, NHS, CPFT, CGL, CRS, Public Health, LAMP, RCE, Mind / WorkWell or AstraZeneca support is assumed unless explicitly confirmed in writing.

GOVERNANCE
Any future work is subject to governance, consent, data protection, safeguarding, qualified clinical leadership and proper commercial / legal review.

THE LINE
OTOS surfaces signals; partner teams make the decisions. That boundary is the design, not a disclaimer bolted on after.
```

```text
Title:
OTOS Angel Brief — Clean Build Review Candidate

Audience:
Angel / strategic funder / high-calibre advisor / early backer.

Purpose:
Secure a serious route-pressure-test conversation and potentially advisory support or a small first cheque.

This is not:
- a hard investment deck;
- a formal raise memo;
- an NHS procurement document;
- an AZ brief;
- a partner letter;
- a generic recovery app pitch.

It is:
- a founder-led Angel / strategic advisory brief;
- a landscape briefing from Dean’s lived proof;
- a request for advice, direction, route-pressure-testing and possible early support.

SOURCE HIERARCHY

1. Original Angel Brief = copy authority.
2. AZ / NIA Master Brief = design/category/style authority.
3. Empty Master Template = build base.
4. Angel image pack = visual assets.
5. v17 = failure reference only.

NON-NEGOTIABLE STORY SPINE

The brief must carry this logic:

1. Untreated or under-treated ADHD can drive self-medication.
2. Self-medication can become addiction.
3. Addiction becomes pain, relapse, despair, family loss, crisis and system cost.
4. Dean lived that pattern.
5. Services saw him repeatedly, but the ADHD/addiction thread was not held.
6. The clinical letter names the mechanism.
7. Once ADHD was treated properly, recovery work finally landed.
8. Dean should not have had to engineer his own rescue route.
9. OTOS exists to test whether this cohort can be found earlier, routed clinically and held between services.
10. NIA is the proposed category name, not an official diagnosis.
11. OTOS is non-clinical infrastructure, not treatment.
12. Partner/service conversations are being prepared, but no endorsement is claimed.
13. The Angel is being asked to help choose the safest first route, not fund everything blindly.

PAGE STRUCTURE — 13 PAGES ONLY

02 The Opportunity

PAGE 01 — COVER

Use Image 01 as full-bleed hero.

Headline:

When ADHD is left waiting,
addiction steps in.
And it does not come quietly.

Design:
- “When ADHD is left waiting,” = white.
- “addiction steps in.” = OTOS orange, largest and most visually central.
- “And it does not come quietly.” = white.
- Large cinematic heading.
- No cramped text.
- No joined words.
- Logo clean and visible.
- Minimal meta only.

Support copy:

Untreated ADHD can become self-medication. Self-medication can become addiction. Addiction becomes pain — relapse, despair, family loss, crisis and system cost.

Dean found a way out by treating the driver underneath. OTOS is the connection he proved in his own case: find the cohort earlier, route the ADHD clinically, and hold the thread until the right support finally lands.

Meta:
Prepared for: A named Angel / strategic funder
From: Dean Butler — Founder
Sent by: Gareth Owen, on Dean’s behalf
Status: Pre-pilot · exploratory · Draft for Review

PAGE 02 — THE OPPORTUNITY

Headline:
How to get Out The Other Side.

Opening copy:

Thank you for asking for more. This brief is short on purpose: a private strategic advisory brief, not a formal investment memorandum.

It is about an unidentified, unacknowledged and high-cost cohort the system already sees and already pays for — adults whose addiction, relapse, repeat A&E contact, family breakdown or service drift may be driven by untreated or under-treated ADHD beneath the visible crisis.

Dean was one of them. His ADHD was already in the story, but it was not held as the driver. After seven years of waiting, he engineered a private route back to proper ADHD treatment. Once medicated correctly, the addiction work, therapy, recovery education and structure finally started to land. That sequence created OTOS.

The opportunity is not another recovery app. It is a bespoke non-clinical infrastructure layer, independent of patient records, that tests whether this cohort can be identified earlier, routed to the right ADHD clinical door, wrapped in continuity, and measured for engagement, relapse risk, follow-through and avoidable cost.

NIA — Neurologically Informed Addiction — is the proposed founder-originated name for the pattern. It is not an official diagnosis. ADHD-driven addiction is the plain-English cohort; NIA is the category signal; OTOS Continuity is the infrastructure that tests whether the missing thread can be held.

The ask is not blanket fundraising. It is a serious route-pressure-test: which first path is safest, fastest and most valuable to prove or stop — Angel/funder, NHS/public, private clinical, AZ/pharma, VCSE ecosystem or staged hybrid?

Six benefit cards only:

SOLVES
Clinically known, operationally lost — seen by many services, held by none.

SAVES
Reactive spend in A&E, detox, rehab, policing, courts, family breakdown and lost productivity.

CONNECTS
ADHD, addiction, alcohol liaison, VCSE, clinical partners and public-health routes around one visible continuity thread.

CREATES
A bounded evidence route for NIA — consent, audit trail, learning report and outcome signals.

INCREASES
Engagement, follow-through, appointment visibility — and the chance recovery work lands in time.

REDUCES
Silent drift, missed handoffs, repeat presentations and unsupported waiting.

PAGE 03 — FOUNDER PROOF

Use Image 03 as wide editorial field or half-page hero.
No framed picture.
No image card.

Headline:
The gap between ADHD and addiction is where people get lost.

Quote:
“I am still on the list. I repeatedly touched every service. Not one of them linked the ADHD and held me correctly.”

Attribution:
Dean Butler, Founder — six A&E presentations, two ICU admissions, three private detoxes.

Copy:
A large share of adults with untreated or under-treated ADHD self-medicate. Many find their way into addiction services; few get the ADHD half identified or treated in time. The gap between “you’re on the list” and “you’re being held” is where drift, relapse and devastation happen. We are already paying for this failure — just in the worst possible places.

Dean recovered by engineering a route the system should have had — creating the conditions for detox, securing the letter, reaching the psychiatrist, restarting ADHD treatment, then watching the recovery work finally land. OTOS exists so the next person does not have to design their own rescue route from inside crisis.

Founder proof is not population proof.

PAGE 04 — CLINICAL ANCHOR / THE COHORT ANCHORED

Use dark proof page style from AZ.

Headline:
The thesis, in clinical language.

Quote:
“Adult ADHD, previously complicated with excess alcohol use to self-medicate inadequately managed ADHD symptoms.”

Attribution:
Dr Fagbemi, Consultant Psychiatrist, Psymplicity — diagnostic letter to the CPFT ADHD Team, April 2025.

Copy:
That sentence is the cohort. Not ADHD and alcohol use treated as two separate problems — adult ADHD, complicated by self-medication. Treating the alcohol alone left the driver underneath untouched; recognising and treating the ADHD is what finally let the rest of the work hold.

The letter is medical correspondence about Dean — not an endorsement of OTOS, and not proof that OTOS works — but it documents that the mechanism is real in a lived pathway.

Standalone authority feature:

THE COHORT, ANCHORED

“Significant and comorbid substance misuse should be managed jointly between general adult and addiction services...”

Royal College of Psychiatrists — ADHD in Adults: Good practice guidance (CR235)

So why is this cohort still drifting between services — and why aren’t we actively holding them while they wait? That is what this demonstrator is built to test.

— Dean, founder, OTOS Continuity™

PAGE 05 — PATHWAY FAILURE

Headline:
The system helped me — off-pathway.

Copy:
The services did help — but they had to help him off-pathway, because the named pathway did not exist. Good people improvised a route the system had not built. Help that depends on improvisation cannot be relied on, or scaled.

A person can be clinically known and still operationally lost — known to services is not the same as held between services. The failure was not bad people. It was the absence of a named route.

And one more truth the recovery makes plain: the work was not wrong — Dean’s brain could not use it yet. Once the ADHD was treated, the same courses, CBT, recovery education and structure became usable. That is the sequence OTOS protects: identify the driver, hold the person while the clinical route catches up, then make sure the right support is there for the moment it can finally land.

Use compact two-column strip only:

NAMED PATHWAY — DID NOT EXIST
No route was designed to hold someone whose ADHD was driving the addiction.

WHAT HAPPENS INSTEAD — DRIFT & REPEAT
Relapse, repeat presentation, A&E, family breakdown — the cost lands everywhere except where it started.

Hard rule:
No huge empty boxes.
No filler cards.
No card larger than needed.

PAGE 06 — ALREADY IN MOTION

Headline:
This is not an idea on paper.
It is a team of services, forming now.

Copy:
You are not being asked to back something speculative and alone. Around this cohort, Dean is building a team of services — and the approaches are now being prepared in writing, in the language of each service, not just talked about.

Partner briefs are prepared or being finalised for the CPFT ADHD Team, CGL & CRS, Alcohol Liaison at Addenbrooke’s, RCE Wellbeing Hub, Mind / WorkWell / therapy, Dr Claire / LAMP and Public Health. Each asks for the right thing from that service: not endorsement, not procurement, not clinical risk — a careful conversation, written support where appropriate, and pressure-testing of the role it could safely play.

Service strip:
CPFT ADHD Team · CGL & CRS · Alcohol Liaison · RCE Wellbeing Hub · Mind / WorkWell / Therapy · Dr Claire / LAMP · Public Health · OTOS Continuity

Compact service cards:
READY NOW
Partner PDFs are being prepared for service-specific written support and pressure-testing.

ADDICTION SIDE
CGL and CRS keyworkers and team leaders already recognise the pattern and have been engaged informally.

CLINICAL SIDE
The diagnostic letter gives the ADHD/self-medication mechanism in clinical language and is being used to progress the NHS pathway.

BEDSIDE SIDE
Alcohol Liaison saw the gap directly: there was nowhere useful to send this pattern once it became visible.

WAITING-WELL SIDE
RCE, Mind / WorkWell, therapy and Dr Claire / LAMP show how active waiting and human connection sit around the clinical pathway.

PUBLIC-HEALTH SIDE
The cost case is framed as discovery, not procurement: can a 50-person, 12-week demonstrator test whether the thread holds?

CGL precedent:
There is already a live precedent on the addiction side: CGL has published practice-development work through Nottingham CVS on ADHD assessment within substance-use services — mapping the very barriers this cohort presents. OTOS does not duplicate that clinical work; it makes continuity visible between the addiction and ADHD pathways, so a cohort already recognised inside addiction settings can be routed and held rather than lost.

Closing line:
No endorsement is claimed, and no partner is being represented as signed up. The point is that the conversation is now teed up — and an advisor or Angel can help shape the safest, fastest first route before the moment is missed.

PAGE 07 — INFRASTRUCTURE

Headline:
The intervention is simple:
make the missing thread visible.

Copy:
OTOS does not replace services. It makes the handoff, waiting period and drift between them visible enough for humans to act.

Waiting is not neutral for this cohort: waiting without structure is where ADHD-driven drift becomes addiction-driven crisis — OTOS turns it into active continuity.

Mechanism table:
OTOS ELEMENT | WHAT IT DOES | WHY IT MATTERS

Micro check-ins | Simple signals between appointments | Shows whether someone is still connected or starting to drift
Transition receipts | Records whether a handoff or referral happened | Stops next steps being assumed when they vanished into silence
Pathway reminders | Prompts before and after moments that slip | Improves follow-through without adding keyworker load
Partner-shaped messages | Agreed messages in partner voice, where consented | Keeps support familiar, human and connected
Drift & silence visibility | Flags no response or missed next step | Makes relapse risk visible earlier
Human review queue | Keeps decisions with people, not automation | Protects clinical boundaries
Tokenised continuity trail | Holds minimal continuity signals only | Pathway visibility without EPR write-back
Learning report | Summarises what happened across the cohort | Creates evidence to continue, pivot or stop

Bottom callout:
The first product is not a cure. It is visibility — seeing whether the person is still held between services before crisis becomes the only signal. VCSE, charity and therapy partners need no NHS record access to take part: consent-led, minimal continuity signals only — no therapy content, no EPR write-back, no new clinical record. OTOS surfaces signals. Partner teams make the decisions.

PAGE 08 — STRATEGIC CROSSROADS

Headline:
Six routes are live.
Which should be tested first?

Copy:
Dean is not pretending the route is already chosen. Six routes are open, each with a different first test — the next real risk is choosing the wrong route first. The Angel is not being asked to choose forever; the Angel is being asked to help pressure-test the first route.

Route table:
A · Angel / funder — First cheque protects founder time + demonstrator — Dilution / wrong early terms — Is this the right first capital, and on what shape?
B · NHS / public — Bounded local demonstrator with governance — Slow procurement; governance load — Is the demonstrator commissionable, and when?
C · Private clinical — ADHD assessment / medication + continuity — Access & equity optics — Does a private partner accelerate or narrow it?
D · AZ / pharma — NIA, medicine-plus-continuity, category — Long cycles; founder distraction — Is the category route worth the time-cost now?
E · VCSE ecosystem — RCE, Mind, WorkWell, Alcohol Liaison, CGL/CRS — Funding fragility — Which partners de-risk the first test?
F · Hybrid — A staged blend of the above — Focus dilution — What is the minimum viable sequence?

Closing:
The near-term test is small either way: a 12-week candidate demonstrator — find the cohort, route to ADHD assessment via qualified channels, wrap in OTOS Continuity, measure candidate change. Small. Surgical. Enough to prove or stop.

PAGE 09 — NATIONAL STAKES / COST CASE

Use Image 09 as wideEditorialField or backgroundAtmosphere.

Headline:
The system is already paying —
in the worst possible places.

Copy:
This is not a new cost. It is a smarter use of money the system is already spending — reactively, repeatedly and across budget lines that rarely speak to each other. The cost does not disappear — it moves to a different budget line. A 50-person, 12-week demonstrator is an evidence budget, not a therapy budget: it exists to replace modelling with locally validated evidence.

Stats:
MACRO BACKDROP
£47bn / year
UK economic cost of alcohol and drugs. HM Government.

THE PRIZE
£17bn
Avoidable annual economic cost of untreated ADHD. NHS ADHD Taskforce, 2025.

LOCAL MODEL
≈£18m / year
Modelled avoidable demand if continuity improves outcomes. OTOS planning estimate — subject to local validation.

PAGE 10 — EVIDENCE / PRIZE / POLICY

Headline:
What getting this right could be worth.

Copy:
The research already says what the founder’s life demonstrated. None of the figures below is an OTOS outcome — they are published evidence candidates and comparators the demonstrator is designed to test locally.

Stats:
23–45%
ADHD prevalence in addiction treatment cohorts
RCPsych

61.3%
Improvement in addictive-disorder outcomes after ADHD diagnosis and treatment
START, 2025

£4.07
Return per £1 in an addiction-continuity comparator
Turning Point / LSE

£17bn
Avoidable annual economic cost of ADHD
ADHD Taskforce 2025

Policy line:
OTOS sits with the direction of travel in national policy — hospital to community, analogue to digital, sickness to prevention. As the planned 250 NHS Community Hubs develop, the missing question is whether the thread between services is held; the DHSC addiction-technology direction matters because this is a fragmentation problem as much as a clinical one.
```

```text
PRIMARY GOAL

Create a premium Angel / strategic advisory briefing that acts like an executive synthesis of the whole OTOS opportunity.

- what the opportunity is;

- why Dean is credible;
- why the timing is urgent;

- a hard investment deck;
- a formal raise memo;

- an AZ pitch;
- a partner letter;
- a generic recovery app pitch.

This is:
- a founder-led Angel / strategic advisory brief;
- a landscape briefing built from Dean’s lived proof;
- an executive synthesis of all route briefs;
- a request for advice, direction, route-pressure-testing and possible early support.

R — ROLE

You are a senior corporate communications director, Angel briefing editor, venture narrative strategist, healthcare innovation copy-governance lead and premium PDF art director.

You must act like someone preparing a document for a serious Angel, strategic funder or high-calibre advisor.

A — ACTION

STEP 1 — SOURCE HIERARCHY

Use sources in this order:

1. Original Angel Brief = copy authority.
   Preserve its directness, warmth and founder-led voice.

2. AZ / NIA Master Brief = visual, category and strategic clarity authority.

   Build from this, not v17.

5. v17 = anti-pattern only.
   Do not copy its layout or components.

STEP 2 — SOURCE EXTRACTION FILTER

Before building, silently extract the best material from all attached briefs using this filter.

Only include material if it helps one of these Angel questions:

Do not include:

- recipient-specific flattery;
- long partner letters;
- internal process notes;
- website-style copy;
- anything that makes endorsement sound secured;
- anything that turns OTOS into a clinical provider.

STEP 3 — NON-NEGOTIABLE STORY SPINE

The brief must carry this logic clearly:

1. Untreated or under-treated ADHD can drive self-medication.
2. Self-medication can become addiction.
3. Addiction becomes pain, relapse, despair, family loss, crisis and system cost.
4. Dean lived that pattern.
5. Services saw him repeatedly, but the ADHD/addiction thread was not held.
6. The clinical letter names the mechanism.
7. Once ADHD was treated properly, recovery work finally landed.
8. Dean should not have had to engineer his own rescue route.
9. OTOS exists to test whether this cohort can be found earlier, routed clinically and held between services.
10. NIA is the proposed category name, not an official diagnosis.
11. OTOS is non-clinical infrastructure, not treatment.
12. Partner/service conversations are being prepared, but no endorsement is claimed.
13. The Angel is being asked to help choose the safest first route, not fund everything blindly.

Do not add a contents page.

STEP 5 — PAGE-BY-PAGE CONTENT

PAGE 01 — COVER

Use Image 01 as full-bleed hero.

Headline:

When ADHD is left waiting,
addiction steps in.
And it does not come quietly.

Design:
- “When ADHD is left waiting,” = white.
- “addiction steps in.” = OTOS orange, largest and most visually central.
- “And it does not come quietly.” = white.
- Large cinematic heading.
- No cramped text.
- No joined words.
- Logo clean and visible.
- Minimal meta only.

Support copy:

Untreated ADHD can become self-medication. Self-medication can become addiction. Addiction becomes pain — relapse, despair, family loss, crisis and system cost.

Dean found a way out by treating the driver underneath. OTOS is the connection he proved in his own case: find the cohort earlier, route the ADHD clinically, and hold the thread until the right support finally lands.

Meta:
Prepared for: A named Angel / strategic funder
From: Dean Butler — Founder
Sent by: Gareth Owen, on Dean’s behalf
Status: Pre-pilot · exploratory · Draft for Review

PAGE 02 — THE OPPORTUNITY

Headline:
How to get Out The Other Side.

Opening copy:

Thank you for asking for more. This brief is short on purpose: a private strategic advisory brief, not a formal investment memorandum.

It is about an unidentified, unacknowledged and high-cost cohort the system already sees and already pays for — adults whose addiction, relapse, repeat A&E contact, family breakdown or service drift may be driven by untreated or under-treated ADHD beneath the visible crisis.

Dean was one of them. His ADHD was already in the story, but it was not held as the driver. After seven years of waiting, he engineered a private route back to proper ADHD treatment. Once medicated correctly, the addiction work, therapy, recovery education and structure finally started to land. That sequence created OTOS.

The opportunity is not another recovery app. It is a bespoke non-clinical infrastructure layer, independent of patient records, that tests whether this cohort can be identified earlier, routed to the right ADHD clinical door, wrapped in continuity, and measured for engagement, relapse risk, follow-through and avoidable cost.

NIA — Neurologically Informed Addiction — is the proposed founder-originated name for the pattern. It is not an official diagnosis. ADHD-driven addiction is the plain-English cohort; NIA is the category signal; OTOS Continuity is the infrastructure that tests whether the missing thread can be held.

The ask is not blanket fundraising. It is a serious route-pressure-test: which first path is safest, fastest and most valuable to prove or stop — Angel/funder, NHS/public, private clinical, AZ/pharma, VCSE ecosystem or staged hybrid?

Six benefit cards only:

SOLVES
Clinically known, operationally lost — seen by many services, held by none.

SAVES
Reactive spend in A&E, detox, rehab, policing, courts, family breakdown and lost productivity.

CONNECTS
ADHD, addiction, alcohol liaison, VCSE, clinical partners and public-health routes around one visible continuity thread.

CREATES
A bounded evidence route for NIA — consent, audit trail, learning report and outcome signals.

INCREASES
Engagement, follow-through, appointment visibility — and the chance recovery work lands in time.

REDUCES
Silent drift, missed handoffs, repeat presentations and unsupported waiting.

PAGE 03 — FOUNDER PROOF

Use the Original Angel voice and strongest lived-proof lines from the Founder / clinical / partner versions.

Use Image 03 as wide editorial field or half-page hero.
No framed picture.
No image card.

Headline:
The gap between ADHD and addiction is where people get lost.

Quote:
“I am still on the list. I repeatedly touched every service. Not one of them linked the ADHD and held me correctly.”

Attribution:
Dean Butler, Founder — six A&E presentations, two ICU admissions, three private detoxes.

Copy:
A large share of adults with untreated or under-treated ADHD self-medicate. Many find their way into addiction services; few get the ADHD half identified or treated in time. The gap between “you’re on the list” and “you’re being held” is where drift, relapse and devastation happen. We are already paying for this failure — just in the worst possible places.

Dean recovered by engineering a route the system should have had — creating the conditions for detox, securing the letter, reaching the psychiatrist, restarting ADHD treatment, then watching the recovery work finally land. OTOS exists so the next person does not have to design their own rescue route from inside crisis.

Founder proof is not population proof.

PAGE 04 — CLINICAL ANCHOR / THE COHORT ANCHORED

Use dark proof page style from AZ.

Headline:
The thesis, in clinical language.

Quote:
“Adult ADHD, previously complicated with excess alcohol use to self-medicate inadequately managed ADHD symptoms.”

Attribution:
Dr Fagbemi, Consultant Psychiatrist, Psymplicity — diagnostic letter to the CPFT ADHD Team, April 2025.

Copy:
That sentence is the cohort. Not ADHD and alcohol use treated as two separate problems — adult ADHD, complicated by self-medication. Treating the alcohol alone left the driver underneath untouched; recognising and treating the ADHD is what finally let the rest of the work hold.

The letter is medical correspondence about Dean — not an endorsement of OTOS, and not proof that OTOS works — but it documents that the mechanism is real in a lived pathway.

Standalone authority feature:

THE COHORT, ANCHORED

“Significant and comorbid substance misuse should be managed jointly between general adult and addiction services...”

Royal College of Psychiatrists — ADHD in Adults: Good practice guidance (CR235)

So why is this cohort still drifting between services — and why aren’t we actively holding them while they wait? That is what this demonstrator is built to test.

— Dean, founder, OTOS Continuity™

PAGE 05 — PATHWAY FAILURE

Headline:
The system helped me — off-pathway.

Copy:
The services did help — but they had to help him off-pathway, because the named pathway did not exist. Good people improvised a route the system had not built. Help that depends on improvisation cannot be relied on, or scaled.

A person can be clinically known and still operationally lost — known to services is not the same as held between services. The failure was not bad people. It was the absence of a named route.

And one more truth the recovery makes plain: the work was not wrong — Dean’s brain could not use it yet. Once the ADHD was treated, the same courses, CBT, recovery education and structure became usable. That is the sequence OTOS protects: identify the driver, hold the person while the clinical route catches up, then make sure the right support is there for the moment it can finally land.

Use compact two-column strip only:

NAMED PATHWAY — DID NOT EXIST
No route was designed to hold someone whose ADHD was driving the addiction.

WHAT HAPPENS INSTEAD — DRIFT & REPEAT
Relapse, repeat presentation, A&E, family breakdown — the cost lands everywhere except where it started.

Hard rule:
No huge empty boxes.
No filler cards.
No card larger than needed.

PAGE 06 — ALREADY IN MOTION / TEAM OF SERVICES

This page should synthesise all partner briefs into one Angel-facing readiness page.

Headline:
This is not an idea on paper.
It is a team of services, forming now.

Copy:
You are not being asked to back something speculative and alone. Around this cohort, Dean is building a team of services — and the approaches are now being prepared in writing, in the language of each service, not just talked about.

Partner briefs are prepared or being finalised for the CPFT ADHD Team, CGL & CRS, Alcohol Liaison at Addenbrooke’s, RCE Wellbeing Hub, Mind / WorkWell / therapy, Dr Claire / LAMP and Public Health. Each asks for the right thing from that service: not endorsement, not procurement, not clinical risk — a careful conversation, written support where appropriate, and pressure-testing of the role it could safely play.

Service strip:
CPFT ADHD Team · CGL & CRS · Alcohol Liaison · RCE Wellbeing Hub · Mind / WorkWell / Therapy · Dr Claire / LAMP · Public Health · OTOS Continuity

Compact service cards:
READY NOW
Partner PDFs are being prepared for service-specific written support and pressure-testing.

ADDICTION SIDE
CGL and CRS keyworkers and team leaders already recognise the pattern and have been engaged informally.

CLINICAL SIDE
The diagnostic letter gives the ADHD/self-medication mechanism in clinical language and is being used to progress the NHS pathway.

BEDSIDE SIDE
Alcohol Liaison saw the gap directly: there was nowhere useful to send this pattern once it became visible.

WAITING-WELL SIDE
RCE, Mind / WorkWell, therapy and Dr Claire / LAMP show how active waiting and human connection sit around the clinical pathway.

PUBLIC-HEALTH SIDE
The cost case is framed as discovery, not procurement: can a 50-person, 12-week demonstrator test whether the thread holds?

CGL precedent:
There is already a live precedent on the addiction side: CGL has published practice-development work through Nottingham CVS on ADHD assessment within substance-use services — mapping the very barriers this cohort presents. OTOS does not duplicate that clinical work; it makes continuity visible between the addiction and ADHD pathways, so a cohort already recognised inside addiction settings can be routed and held rather than lost.

Closing line:
No endorsement is claimed, and no partner is being represented as signed up. The point is that the conversation is now teed up — and an advisor or Angel can help shape the safest, fastest first route before the moment is missed.

PAGE 07 — INFRASTRUCTURE

Headline:
The intervention is simple:
make the missing thread visible.

Copy:
OTOS does not replace services. It makes the handoff, waiting period and drift between them visible enough for humans to act.

Waiting is not neutral for this cohort: waiting without structure is where ADHD-driven drift becomes addiction-driven crisis — OTOS turns it into active continuity.

Mechanism table:
OTOS ELEMENT | WHAT IT DOES | WHY IT MATTERS

Micro check-ins | Simple signals between appointments | Shows whether someone is still connected or starting to drift
Transition receipts | Records whether a handoff or referral happened | Stops next steps being assumed when they vanished into silence
Pathway reminders | Prompts before and after moments that slip | Improves follow-through without adding keyworker load
Partner-shaped messages | Agreed messages in partner voice, where consented | Keeps support familiar, human and connected
Drift & silence visibility | Flags no response or missed next step | Makes relapse risk visible earlier
Human review queue | Keeps decisions with people, not automation | Protects clinical boundaries
Tokenised continuity trail | Holds minimal continuity signals only | Pathway visibility without EPR write-back
Learning report | Summarises what happened across the cohort | Creates evidence to continue, pivot or stop

Bottom callout:
The first product is not a cure. It is visibility — seeing whether the person is still held between services before crisis becomes the only signal. VCSE, charity and therapy partners need no NHS record access to take part: consent-led, minimal continuity signals only — no therapy content, no EPR write-back, no new clinical record. OTOS surfaces signals. Partner teams make the decisions.

PAGE 08 — STRATEGIC CROSSROADS

Headline:
Six routes are live.
Which should be tested first?

Copy:
Dean is not pretending the route is already chosen. Six routes are open, each with a different first test — the next real risk is choosing the wrong route first. The Angel is not being asked to choose forever; the Angel is being asked to help pressure-test the first route.

Route table:
A · Angel / funder — First cheque protects founder time + demonstrator — Dilution / wrong early terms — Is this the right first capital, and on what shape?
B · NHS / public — Bounded local demonstrator with governance — Slow procurement; governance load — Is the demonstrator commissionable, and when?
C · Private clinical — ADHD assessment / medication + continuity — Access & equity optics — Does a private partner accelerate or narrow it?
D · AZ / pharma — NIA, medicine-plus-continuity, category — Long cycles; founder distraction — Is the category route worth the time-cost now?
E · VCSE ecosystem — RCE, Mind, WorkWell, Alcohol Liaison, CGL/CRS — Funding fragility — Which partners de-risk the first test?
F · Hybrid — A staged blend of the above — Focus dilution — What is the minimum viable sequence?

Closing:
The near-term test is small either way: a 12-week candidate demonstrator — find the cohort, route to ADHD assessment via qualified channels, wrap in OTOS Continuity, measure candidate change. Small. Surgical. Enough to prove or stop.

PAGE 09 — NATIONAL STAKES / COST CASE

Use the strongest cost-case language from Public Health / AZ only if it remains Angel-relevant.

Headline:
The system is already paying —
in the worst possible places.

Copy:
This is not a new cost. It is a smarter use of money the system is already spending — reactively, repeatedly and across budget lines that rarely speak to each other. The cost does not disappear — it moves to a different budget line. A 50-person, 12-week demonstrator is an evidence budget, not a therapy budget: it exists to replace modelling with locally validated evidence.

Stats:
MACRO BACKDROP
£47bn / year
UK economic cost of alcohol and drugs. HM Government.

THE PRIZE
£17bn
Avoidable annual economic cost of untreated ADHD. NHS ADHD Taskforce, 2025.

LOCAL MODEL
≈£18m / year
Modelled avoidable demand if continuity improves outcomes. OTOS planning estimate — subject to local validation.

PAGE 10 — EVIDENCE / PRIZE / POLICY

Headline:
What getting this right could be worth.

Copy:
The research already says what the founder’s life demonstrated. None of the figures below is an OTOS outcome — they are published evidence candidates and comparators the demonstrator is designed to test locally.

Stats:
23–45%
ADHD prevalence in addiction treatment cohorts
RCPsych

61.3%
Improvement in addictive-disorder outcomes after ADHD diagnosis and treatment
START, 2025

£4.07
Return per £1 in an addiction-continuity comparator
Turning Point / LSE

£17bn
Avoidable annual economic cost of ADHD
ADHD Taskforce 2025

Policy line:
OTOS sits with the direction of travel in national policy — hospital to community, analogue to digital, sickness to prevention. As the planned 250 NHS Community Hubs develop, the missing question is whether the thread between services is held; the DHSC addiction-technology direction matters because this is a fragmentation problem as much as a clinical one.

PAGE 11 — WHAT SUPPORT UNLOCKS

This page must be clean and investor-readable.

Headline:
What a first cheque — or your advice — would unlock.

Opening:
A small first cheque or strategic advisory support, sized to reach a credible proof-of-question over the next 9–12 months. Structure is open — SAFE, advance subscription, structured grant-with-rights or angel-syndicate — shaped by the conversation. This is not yet a formal raise, and there is no valuation or fixed terms on the table.

Angel opportunity line:
This is the Angel opportunity: not just a service idea, but a new category, a new intervention model and a new infrastructure layer. NIA is not yet a market — that is why the first proof point matters.

Funding line:
Working demonstrator budget is expected to sit in the £90k–£150k range, subject to final costing, governance and partner route.

Two-column layout:

LEFT COLUMN — WHAT SUPPORT UNLOCKS
- Protected founder time and a refined demonstrator design.
- Governance and data-protection preparation.
- Qualified clinical-partner scoping.
- Service-specific written-support requests drawn from the partner briefs.
- Evidence pack and route comparison.

RIGHT COLUMN — FRICTION & BLIND SPOTS
- Dean cannot execute all routes at once — founder time is the bottleneck.
- Clinical governance must be shaped properly before any test.
- The first demonstrator route must be chosen carefully.
- Partner interest must become written support without overstating endorsement.
- NIA is powerful but must be evidenced safely.
- The right advisor helps choose the framework, not dictate the vision.

PAGE 12 — BOUNDARY / GOVERNANCE

Headline:
Stated plainly,
held to.

Six balanced cards:
STATUS
OTOS is early-stage, pre-pilot and non-clinical. NIA is proposed founder-originated terminology — not an official diagnosis.

WHAT OTOS DOES NOT DO
Does not diagnose ADHD, prescribe, manage medication, treat addiction, deliver therapy, replace any NHS / CGL / CPFT / CRS / GP / psychiatry / recovery / VCSE / public-health / crisis service, make clinical decisions, or act as an EPR.

HOW EVIDENCE IS FRAMED
Founder proof is not population proof. Published figures are evidence candidates, not OTOS outcomes. The clinical letter is medical correspondence, not an endorsement. Modelled figures are potential, not guaranteed savings.

PARTNER STATUS
No partner endorsement, approved pilot, procurement, NHS, CPFT, CGL, CRS, Public Health, LAMP, RCE, Mind / WorkWell or AstraZeneca support is assumed unless explicitly confirmed in writing.

GOVERNANCE
Any future work is subject to governance, consent, data protection, safeguarding, qualified clinical leadership and proper commercial / legal review.

THE LINE
OTOS surfaces signals; partner teams make the decisions. That boundary is the design, not a disclaimer bolted on after.

PAGE 13 — NEXT STEP

Headline:
Which route should we test first?

Copy:
If any of this is interesting, the next step is simple: a 30-minute exploratory call with Dean and Gareth. No deck, no pitch theatre — just the founder, the mechanism, the candidate demonstrator, the partner-readiness position, and an honest conversation about which route to test first.

LEVEL 1 — Advisory call
Pressure-test the route and the category — 30 minutes with Dean and Gareth.
```

### Assistant output 127 — 2026-06-23T22:14:44.197418+00:00 — gpt-5-5-thinking

You’re right to lock this down now. **No, don’t save everything loose in `/Users/user`**. That is your Home folder. The safe structure is:

`/Users/user/Downloads` = all downloads  
`/Users/user/Desktop/_Screenshots` = all screenshots  
`/Users/user/Documents` = proper documents  
`/Users/user/Desktop` = only active/current working folders, not a dumping ground

Run this. It will **not delete anything**. It will:

1. Create a screenshots folder on your Desktop.
2. Make screenshots land there instead of loose on Desktop.
3. Try to force Safari/Chrome/Edge/Brave/Arc downloads back to local `~/Downloads`.
4. Create a Desktop inventory report so we can see what folders are there.

```zsh
cd ~/Downloads
cat > mac-location-reset-and-desktop-inventory.zsh <<'ZSH'
#!/bin/zsh
set -euo pipefail

HOME_DIR="$HOME"
DOWNLOADS_DIR="$HOME_DIR/Downloads"
DESKTOP_DIR="$HOME_DIR/Desktop"
SCREENSHOT_DIR="$DESKTOP_DIR/_Screenshots"
STAMP="$(date '+%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S')"
REPORT="$DESKTOP_DIR/Desktop_Inventory_$STAMP.txt"

mkdir -p "$DOWNLOADS_DIR"
mkdir -p "$SCREENSHOT_DIR"

echo "1) Setting screenshots to: $SCREENSHOT_DIR"
defaults write com.apple.screencapture location "$SCREENSHOT_DIR"
defaults write com.apple.screencapture type -string png
killall SystemUIServer 2>/dev/null || true

echo "2) Setting Safari downloads to: $DOWNLOADS_DIR"
defaults write com.apple.Safari DownloadsPath -string "$DOWNLOADS_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true

echo "3) Setting Chromium-based browser download folders where preference files exist..."
python3 <<'PY'
import json
import os
import pathlib
import shutil
from datetime import datetime

home = pathlib.Path.home()
downloads = str(home / "Downloads")
stamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S")

prefs = [
    home / "Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome/Default/Preferences",
    home / "Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome/Profile 1/Preferences",
    home / "Library/Application Support/Microsoft Edge/Default/Preferences",
    home / "Library/Application Support/BraveSoftware/Brave-Browser/Default/Preferences",
    home / "Library/Application Support/Arc/User Data/Default/Preferences",
    home / "Library/Application Support/Chromium/Default/Preferences",
]

for path in prefs:
    if not path.exists():
        continue

    try:
        backup = path.with_name(path.name + f".backup_{stamp}")
        shutil.copy2(path, backup)

        data = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
        data.setdefault("download", {})
        data["download"]["default_directory"] = downloads
        data["download"]["prompt_for_download"] = False

        path.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding="utf-8")
        print(f"Updated: {path}")
        print(f"Backup:  {backup}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Could not update {path}: {e}")
PY

echo "4) Creating Desktop inventory report..."

python3 <<PY
import os
import pathlib
import time

desktop = pathlib.Path("$DESKTOP_DIR")
report = pathlib.Path("$REPORT")

IGNORE = {".DS_Store"}

def human_size(num):
    for unit in ["B", "KB", "MB", "GB", "TB"]:
        if num < 1024:
            return f"{num:.1f}{unit}" if unit != "B" else f"{int(num)}B"
        num /= 1024
    return f"{num:.1f}PB"

def safe_stat(path):
    try:
        return path.stat()
    except Exception:
        return None

def folder_summary(path):
    total = 0
    files = 0
    folders = 0
    newest = 0

    for root, dirnames, filenames in os.walk(path, topdown=True):
        root_path = pathlib.Path(root)

        # avoid some noisy hidden/system caches if present
        dirnames[:] = [d for d in dirnames if d not in {".Trash", ".Spotlight-V100"}]

        folders += len(dirnames)

        for name in filenames:
            p = root_path / name
            st = safe_stat(p)
            if not st:
                continue
            files += 1
            total += st.st_size
            newest = max(newest, st.st_mtime)

    return total, files, folders, newest

items = []
for item in desktop.iterdir():
    if item.name in IGNORE:
        continue
    try:
        if item.is_dir():
            size, files, folders, newest = folder_summary(item)
            kind = "FOLDER"
        else:
            st = item.stat()
            size, files, folders, newest = st.st_size, 1, 0, st.st_mtime
            kind = "FILE"
        items.append((item.name, kind, size, files, folders, newest, item))
    except Exception as e:
        items.append((item.name, "ERROR", 0, 0, 0, 0, item))

items.sort(key=lambda x: (x[1] != "FOLDER", -x[2], x[0].lower()))

with report.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    f.write("MAC DESKTOP INVENTORY\\n")
    f.write("=" * 80 + "\\n")
    f.write(f"Created: {time.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}\\n")
    f.write(f"Desktop: {desktop}\\n")
    f.write(f"Downloads: {pathlib.Path.home() / 'Downloads'}\\n")
    f.write(f"Screenshots: {pathlib.Path.home() / 'Desktop' / '_Screenshots'}\\n")
    f.write("=" * 80 + "\\n\\n")

    f.write("TOP-LEVEL DESKTOP ITEMS\\n")
    f.write("-" * 80 + "\\n")
    for name, kind, size, files, folders, newest, path in items:
        date = time.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M", time.localtime(newest)) if newest else "unknown"
        f.write(f"{kind:7} | {human_size(size):>9} | files {files:5} | folders {folders:4} | newest {date} | {name}\\n")

    f.write("\\n\\nFOLDER CONTENT PREVIEWS\\n")
    f.write("=" * 80 + "\\n")

    for name, kind, size, files, folders, newest, path in items:
        if kind != "FOLDER":
            continue

        f.write(f"\\n## {name}\\n")
        f.write(f"Size: {human_size(size)} | Files: {files} | Folders: {folders}\\n")

        try:
            children = sorted(path.iterdir(), key=lambda p: (not p.is_dir(), p.name.lower()))
            shown = 0
            for child in children:
                if child.name in IGNORE:
                    continue
                label = "DIR " if child.is_dir() else "FILE"
                st = safe_stat(child)
                csize = human_size(st.st_size) if st and child.is_file() else ""
                f.write(f"  - {label} {child.name} {csize}\\n")
                shown += 1
                if shown >= 30:
                    remaining = max(0, len(children) - shown)
                    f.write(f"  ... plus about {remaining} more items\\n")
                    break
        except Exception as e:
            f.write(f"  Could not read folder: {e}\\n")

print(report)
PY

echo ""
echo "DONE."
echo "Screenshots now land here:"
echo "$SCREENSHOT_DIR"
echo ""
echo "Desktop inventory report created here:"
echo "$REPORT"
echo ""
echo "IMPORTANT:"
echo "- Quit and reopen Safari/Chrome/Edge/Brave/Arc for download-folder changes to stick."
echo "- For apps that remember the last save location, save once into Downloads and they should follow."
ZSH

chmod +x mac-location-reset-and-desktop-inventory.zsh
zsh mac-location-reset-and-desktop-inventory.zsh
```

After it runs, open the new file on your Desktop called something like:

`Desktop_Inventory_2026-06-23_XX-XX-XX.txt`

Paste the **TOP-LEVEL DESKTOP ITEMS** section here and I’ll tell you exactly what’s on the Desktop, what looks safe to archive, what should stay, and what folders look suspicious/duplicated.

```zsh
import os
import json
import pathlib
import shutil
import subprocess
import time
from datetime import datetime

home = pathlib.Path.home()
desktop = home / "Desktop"
downloads = home / "Downloads"
screenshots = desktop / "_Screenshots"
stamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S")
report = desktop / f"Desktop_Inventory_{stamp}.txt"

downloads.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
screenshots.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

print(f"Setting screenshots folder to: {screenshots}")
subprocess.run(["defaults", "write", "com.apple.screencapture", "location", str(screenshots)])
subprocess.run(["defaults", "write", "com.apple.screencapture", "type", "-string", "png"])
subprocess.run(["killall", "SystemUIServer"], stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL)

print(f"Setting Safari downloads folder to: {downloads}")
subprocess.run(["defaults", "write", "com.apple.Safari", "DownloadsPath", "-string", str(downloads)])

print("Setting browser download folders where preference files exist...")
prefs = [
    home / "Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome/Default/Preferences",
    home / "Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome/Profile 1/Preferences",
    home / "Library/Application Support/Microsoft Edge/Default/Preferences",
    home / "Library/Application Support/BraveSoftware/Brave-Browser/Default/Preferences",
    home / "Library/Application Support/Arc/User Data/Default/Preferences",
    home / "Library/Application Support/Chromium/Default/Preferences",
]

for path in prefs:
    if not path.exists():
        continue
    try:
        backup = path.with_name(path.name + f".backup_{stamp}")
        shutil.copy2(path, backup)
        data = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
        data.setdefault("download", {})
        data["download"]["default_directory"] = str(downloads)
        data["download"]["prompt_for_download"] = False
        path.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding="utf-8")
        print(f"Updated: {path}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Could not update {path}: {e}")

def human_size(num):
    for unit in ["B", "KB", "MB", "GB", "TB"]:
        if num < 1024:
            return f"{num:.1f}{unit}" if unit != "B" else f"{int(num)}B"
        num /= 1024
    return f"{num:.1f}PB"

def safe_stat(path):
    try:
        return path.stat()
    except Exception:
        return None

def folder_summary(path):
    total = 0
    files = 0
    folders = 0
    newest = 0

        root_path = pathlib.Path(root)

            p = root_path / name
            st = safe_stat(p)
            if not st:
                continue
            files += 1
            total += st.st_size
            newest = max(newest, st.st_mtime)
    return total, files, folders, newest

items = []
for item in desktop.iterdir():
    if item.name == ".DS_Store":
        continue
    try:
        if item.is_dir():
            size, files, folders, newest = folder_summary(item)
            kind = "FOLDER"

            kind = "FILE"
        items.append((item.name, kind, size, files, folders, newest, item))
    except Exception:
        items.append((item.name, "ERROR", 0, 0, 0, 0, item))

items.sort(key=lambda x: (x[1] != "FOLDER", -x[2], x[0].lower()))

with report.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    f.write("MAC DESKTOP INVENTORY\n")

                st = safe_stat(child)
                csize = human_size(st.st_size) if st and child.is_file() else ""
                f.write(f"  - {label} {child.name} {csize}\n")
                shown += 1
                if shown >= 30:
                    f.write(f"  ... plus about {max(0, len(children) - shown)} more items\n")
                    break
        except Exception as e:
            f.write(f"  Could not read folder: {e}\n")

print("")
print("DONE")
print(f"Screenshots now land here: {screenshots}")
print(f"Desktop inventory report created here: {report}")
print("")
print("Quit and reopen Chrome/Safari/Arc/Edge/Brave for download-folder changes to stick.")
PY
```

Then open the Desktop file called:

```text
Desktop_Inventory_YYYY-MM-DD_XX-XX-XX.txt
```

Paste me just the section called **TOP-LEVEL DESKTOP ITEMS** and I’ll tell you what those folders are and what should stay/move/archive.
